verification.payload:
tree 6dbcb4c7f8a0e5c5b4c6d100573c5246cbefe132
parent 56ccd0ddedac64a80a0ceabe1464f36e24b28627
author Rob Thelen <rgthelen@gmail.com> 1772141149 -0600
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772141149 -0600

feat: vision API improvements, evaluation pipeline, and object tracking (#780)

* feat(vision): detect+classify combo, session TTL cleanup, COCO dataset support

1. POST /v1/vision/detect_classify — YOLO detect → crop each bbox → CLIP
   classify per crop in a single round-trip. Eliminates 3+ API calls for
   the most common multi-model pattern.

2. Streaming session TTL cleanup — orphaned sessions auto-expire after 60s
   of no frames. Background cleanup task runs every 15s. New endpoint
   GET /v1/vision/stream/sessions lists active sessions with idle time.

3. COCO JSON dataset support — training endpoint auto-detects COCO format
   and converts to YOLO internally before training. Supports COCO bbox
   format ([x,y,w,h] absolute → normalized center format), train/val
   splitting, and generates data.yaml for ultralytics.

* fix: move start_session_cleanup() into lifespan (needs running event loop)

asyncio.create_task() requires a running event loop. Calling it at module
level caused RuntimeError during import and broke test collection.

* fix: RGBA crop mode, COCO path traversal, empty classes validation

- Convert non-RGB crops to RGB before JPEG encoding (fixes RGBA/P/L crash)
- Sanitize COCO file_name to basename, reject path traversal chars
- Resolve-based containment check for image source paths
- Reject empty classes list in server proxy with 422
- Remove unused numpy import

* feat(vision): model evaluation pipeline with auto-eval/promote

Add vision model evaluation endpoints:
- POST /v1/vision/eval — evaluate model against dataset (async job)
- GET /v1/vision/eval/{job_id} — poll eval status/results
- POST /v1/vision/eval/compare — head-to-head comparison
- GET /v1/vision/eval/leaderboard — ranked model leaderboard
- GET /v1/vision/eval/leaderboard/{model_name} — model history

Core features:
- ModelEvaluator wraps ultralytics .val() with proper COCO metrics
- Actual size-based recall (small/medium/large objects) from predictions
- Configurable weighted composite scoring (mAP, recall, speed, F1)
- SQLite-backed leaderboard at ~/.llamafarm/models/vision/eval.db
- EvalDB with WAL mode, indexes, promotion tracking

Training integration:
- auto_eval flag on /v1/vision/train — eval on completion
- auto_promote flag — promote if score beats leaderboard best
- on_complete callback in IncrementalTrainer
- Scoring weights configurable per-request or via pipeline.json

13 unit tests passing (scoring, DB, compare, roundtrips).

* feat(server): eval proxy routes + route ordering fix

Add server-side proxy for vision eval endpoints (:14345 → :11540):
- POST /v1/vision/eval (Form data)
- POST /v1/vision/eval/compare (Form data)
- GET /v1/vision/eval/leaderboard
- GET /v1/vision/eval/leaderboard/{model_name}
- GET /v1/vision/eval/{job_id}

Fix route shadowing: specific paths (compare, leaderboard) must be
defined before parameterized {job_id} path in both runtime and server.

VisionEvalService proxies via UniversalRuntimeService._make_request().

10/10 integration tests passing through full proxy chain.

* fix(eval): SQLite thread safety + real error messages

- Add check_same_thread=False to EvalDB connection — evaluate() runs
  in asyncio.to_thread() worker, but DB was created in main thread
- Return actual exception string in job error field instead of generic
  'Evaluation failed' — eval jobs are internal ML debugging tools,
  detailed errors are needed for troubleshooting
- Add exc_info=True to error logging for full tracebacks

Tested: eval job completed successfully against VisDrone dataset
(mAP50=0.213, score=0.304, 12s eval time on CPU)

* feat(eval): subprocess isolation for eval jobs

Eval jobs now run in isolated child processes via eval_worker.py.
If an eval OOMs or crashes, the child dies but the runtime stays alive.

Architecture:
- eval_worker.py: standalone script, runs ModelEvaluator._evaluate_sync()
- Router spawns worker via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec()
- Results communicated via temp JSON file
- Worker inherits TRANSFORMERS_SKIP_MPS=1 from env
- Temp files cleaned up in finally blocks

Both _run_eval and _run_compare use subprocess isolation.
auto_eval_after_training also uses subprocess.

Verified: eval completes with correct metrics (mAP50=0.213),
runtime stays healthy during and after eval, full proxy chain
(:14345 → :11540) working. 7/7 integration + 13/13 unit tests.

* fix(vision): add model name validation to detect_classify proxy

* fix: address all review bot comments on PR #778

Security:
- _resolve_model_path: constrain .pt paths to ~/.llamafarm or cwd,
  reject traversal chars, resolve-based containment check
- trainer on_complete: validate model_id (basename, no traversal)
- URL-encode dataset/model_name in eval_service proxy URLs
- Forward auto_promote flag from server proxy to runtime
- Generic error messages in job responses (detailed errors logged)

Concurrency:
- threading.Lock on EvalDB write operations (save, record_promotion)
  for safe concurrent access with check_same_thread=False

Logic:
- auto_promote implies auto_eval (don't silently ignore)
- Guard temp file cleanup against UnboundLocalError (init to None)

Cleanup:
- Remove unused imports: re (eval_model), DEFAULT_WEIGHTS + _eval_lock
  (evaluation.py), json + sqlite3 + DEFAULT_WEIGHTS (test_eval.py),
  os (eval_worker.py)
- Use tmp_path fixture instead of hardcoded /tmp in tests

* fix: escape MDX curly braces in vision-eval docs

Docusaurus MDX interprets {job_id}, {model_name}, {name}, {n} as
JSX expressions. Escape with backslashes to render as literal text.

* feat: object tracking with persistent IDs via ultralytics model.track()

4 endpoints: start, frame, status, stop
- ByteTrack/BoT-SORT/OC-SORT via ultralytics model.track(persist=True)
- Each session holds its own YOLO model with tracker state
- Start+first-frame combo (optional image on start)
- Session TTL auto-cleanup (120s idle)
- Max 50 concurrent sessions
- Path traversal / containment checks on model resolution
- Server proxy (Form data) + runtime (JSON)
- 14 unit tests, 6/6 live integration tests through proxy chain

Runtime: ~380 lines | Server proxy: ~90 lines | Tests: ~150 lines

* chore: add lapx dependency for ByteTrack/BoT-SORT tracking

* docs: vision object tracking API reference

Covers all 4 endpoints, tracker algorithms, session management,
model resolution, dependencies, and Python examples.

* fix: add CodeQL suppression comments for path-injection false positives

All paths validated: reject .., \, : + basename-only model names +
resolve() + is_relative_to() containment. CodeQL doesn't track through
is_relative_to() so flags as py/path-injection.

* fix: auto-detect MPS/CUDA for training instead of defaulting to CPU

Was hardcoded to device='cpu'. Now detects MPS (Apple Silicon GPU),
CUDA, or falls back to CPU. Significant speedup on Mac.

* fix: make imgsz and patience configurable in training API

Both were hardcoded (imgsz=640, patience=50). Now configurable via:
- TrainingConfig dataclass (defaults preserved)
- TrainConfigRequest (API validation: imgsz 320-2560, patience 0-500)
- Server proxy Form params

Allows training at 1280px for higher-quality models.

* fix: sequential training job queue — prevent concurrent OOM

Training jobs now run one at a time via asyncio.Queue + worker.
Previously each job got its own asyncio.Task, causing concurrent
model loading that OOM'd at ~46GB on Mac.

- asyncio.Queue with single _job_worker coroutine
- Jobs queued as QUEUED, worker processes sequentially
- Cancel marks job as CANCELLED, worker skips it
- wait_for_job polls status instead of awaiting task

* test: add unit tests for COCO converter, detect+classify, streaming sessions

Addresses Matt's review feedback on missing test coverage:
- 17 COCO converter tests (path traversal, bbox normalization, split ratio, validation)
- 8 detect+classify tests (RGBA handling, tiny crop skip, empty classes, full flow)
- 13 streaming session tests (lifecycle, TTL cleanup, cascade, cooldown, max sessions)

38/38 passing

* fix: detect basename collisions in COCO converter

When different COCO subdirectories contain files with the same basename,
the second file is now skipped with a warning instead of silently
overwriting the first. Addresses cubic review feedback.

* style: fix ruff E702/B007 in test files

* fix: address review bot issues on PR #780

Tracking fixes:
- YOLO model load moved to asyncio.to_thread (was blocking event loop)
- Per-session asyncio.Lock prevents concurrent frame races on tracker state
- Ruff auto-fixes applied

Training fixes:
- cancel_job now supports RUNNING jobs via YOLO epoch-end callback
- Cancellation callback raises KeyboardInterrupt at epoch boundary
- Dedicated except handler for cancelled vs failed jobs
- Clear docstring on queued vs running cancellation behavior

- Remove unused textwrap import, prefix unused title/tags vars (generate_sdk.py)
- Fix duplicate method names: prefix non-GET methods with HTTP verb (generate_sdk.py)
- Regenerate SDK methods with fixed generator
- Ruff check + format on all changed files

  to Selector for compact filters and inline table-cell selects

No behavior changes. All dropdowns function identically.

* fix(designer): add focus-visible ring styling to Selector triggers

commit.message:
feat(vision): Vision MVP — detect, classify, train, cascade, feedback (#765)

* feat(vision): add vision MVP — detect, classify, train, cascade, feedback

Vision pipeline MVP with 7 capabilities:
- YOLO object detection (yolov8n/s/m/l/x, yolov11n/s/m)
- CLIP zero-shot classification (clip-vit-base/large, siglip)
- Incremental training with versioned model storage
- Auto ONNX export after training (Android-ready)
- Cascade fallover (2-hop, local or remote URL)
- Replay buffer with ModelOpinion for feedback cycle
- Pipeline config as JSON, Atmosphere-ready remote refs

Runtime layer:
- models/vision_base.py — abstract base classes
- models/yolo_model.py — YOLO wrapper via ultralytics
- models/clip_model.py — CLIP wrapper via transformers
- routers/vision/ — detect, classify, train, models, streaming
- storage/image_store.py — SQLite image metadata
- vision_training/ — IncrementalTrainer, ReplayBuffer

Server layer:
- services/vision/ — proxy services using _make_request()
- api/routers/vision/ — 14 endpoints proxying to runtime

Storage: ~/.llamafarm/models/vision/{model_id}/
  v{N}.pt, v{N}.onnx, current.pt, current.onnx,
  training_data/, replay_buffer.db, pipeline.json

Tested: 17/17 end-to-end integration tests passing
  (examples/vision_mvp_demo.py)

* fix(vision): address review findings — lint, cache corruption, CLIP edge cases, WAL mode

* fix(vision): lint cleanup, SSRF mitigation, path traversal guard, version collision fix

* fix(vision): address review bot findings — security hardening + bug fixes

- Strengthen path traversal protection in model save/load (Path.name validation)
- YOLO model path validation (reject '..' in paths)
- Use get_data_dir() for VISION_MODELS_DIR instead of Path.home()
- CLIP: add asyncio.Lock for class embedding updates (race condition)
- CLIP: validate empty classes list, cap top_k to num_classes
- Replay buffer: skip eviction when updating existing sample
- base64 decode with validate=True for strict input checking
- Add explanatory comments to contextlib.suppress blocks

* fix(vision): strengthen path validation + address remaining code quality comments

- YOLO: resolve-based containment check (must be within ~/.llamafarm or cwd)
- YOLO: basename-only validation for dynamic model IDs
- Models router: reject Windows drive letters, backslashes, resolve containment
- Server: validate model_id before path construction in load_detection_model
- Remove unused Path import from server.py
- Add explanatory comments to all suppress/empty-except blocks

---------

Co-authored-by: Matt Hamann <mhamann@rownd.io>

feat(vision): Vision MVP — detect, classify, train, cascade, feedback (#765)

* feat(vision): add vision MVP — detect, classify, train, cascade, feedback

Vision pipeline MVP with 7 capabilities:
- YOLO object detection (yolov8n/s/m/l/x, yolov11n/s/m)
- CLIP zero-shot classification (clip-vit-base/large, siglip)
- Incremental training with versioned model storage
- Auto ONNX export after training (Android-ready)
- Cascade fallover (2-hop, local or remote URL)
- Replay buffer with ModelOpinion for feedback cycle
- Pipeline config as JSON, Atmosphere-ready remote refs

Runtime layer:
- models/vision_base.py — abstract base classes
- models/yolo_model.py — YOLO wrapper via ultralytics
- models/clip_model.py — CLIP wrapper via transformers
- routers/vision/ — detect, classify, train, models, streaming
- storage/image_store.py — SQLite image metadata
- vision_training/ — IncrementalTrainer, ReplayBuffer

Server layer:
- services/vision/ — proxy services using _make_request()
- api/routers/vision/ — 14 endpoints proxying to runtime

Storage: ~/.llamafarm/models/vision/{model_id}/
  v{N}.pt, v{N}.onnx, current.pt, current.onnx,
  training_data/, replay_buffer.db, pipeline.json

Tested: 17/17 end-to-end integration tests passing
  (examples/vision_mvp_demo.py)

* fix(vision): address review findings — lint, cache corruption, CLIP edge cases, WAL mode

* fix(vision): lint cleanup, SSRF mitigation, path traversal guard, version collision fix

* fix(vision): address review bot findings — security hardening + bug fixes

- Strengthen path traversal protection in model save/load (Path.name validation)
- YOLO model path validation (reject '..' in paths)
- Use get_data_dir() for VISION_MODELS_DIR instead of Path.home()
- CLIP: add asyncio.Lock for class embedding updates (race condition)
- CLIP: validate empty classes list, cap top_k to num_classes
- Replay buffer: skip eviction when updating existing sample
- base64 decode with validate=True for strict input checking
- Add explanatory comments to contextlib.suppress blocks

* fix(vision): strengthen path validation + address remaining code quality comments

- YOLO: resolve-based containment check (must be within ~/.llamafarm or cwd)
- YOLO: basename-only validation for dynamic model IDs
- Models router: reject Windows drive letters, backslashes, resolve containment
- Server: validate model_id before path construction in load_detection_model
- Remove unused Path import from server.py
- Add explanatory comments to all suppress/empty-except blocks

commit.message:
chore: bump vulnerable dependencies across monorepo (#764)

* chore: bump vulnerable dependencies across monorepo

Bump minimum versions for packages with known security vulnerabilities
flagged by Dependabot. Regenerate all lockfiles and run uv sync/npm install.

Python:
- pillow >=12.1.1 (CVE: out-of-bounds write on PSD images)
- protobuf >=6.33.5 (JSON recursion depth bypass)
- python-multipart >=0.0.22 (arbitrary file write)
- filelock >=3.20.3 (TOCTOU symlink vulnerability)
- virtualenv >=20.36.1 (TOCTOU in directory creation)
- torch >=2.6.0 (torch.load RCE, critical)
- aiohttp >=3.13.3, urllib3 >=2.6.3, starlette >=0.49.1

npm:
- axios ^1.13.5 (DoS via __proto__ key in mergeConfig)

Also adds pillow override in runtime to bypass surya-ocr's
pillow<11 cap, and drops Intel Mac torch override (PyTorch
dropped Intel Mac support after 2.2.2).

* fix: relax filelock pin to fix binary E2E resolution failure

The PyTorch CPU index only has filelock<=3.20.0. When the PyApp binary
runs uv with UV_EXTRA_INDEX_URL pointing to the PyTorch index, uv's
default first-match strategy resolves filelock from there and can't
satisfy >=3.20.3. Relax to >=3.16.1 which the PyTorch index can satisfy.

* chore: update uv lock

commit.message:
chore: automated changelog docs with AI-generated prose (#751)

* feat(docs): add automated changelog docs with AI-generated prose

- Add workflow to generate human-readable release notes on release PRs
- Add prose-changelog action that transforms conventional commits to prose
- Add changelog page to docs site with expandable release sections
- Add manual script for backfilling or recovering from failures

The workflow triggers on release-please PRs, generates prose using
LlamaFarm's AI capabilities, and commits the updated docs back to the PR.

* fix: address PR review feedback for changelog automation

- Add step to get PR branch for workflow_dispatch events
- Fix version existence check to use specific pattern (avoid v1.2 vs v1.20)
- Fix unquoted pr_number in JS expressions (handle empty inputs)

* fix: update script and workflow for accordion-based changelog

- Rewrite update-changelog-docs.sh to add accordions instead of table rows
- Use getline in awk for safer file content insertion
- Remove individual file creation (now using single index.md with accordions)

* fix(docs): update changelog docs and format improvements

Updates documentation to reflect individual file-based release structure,
improves table formatting in llms-full.txt, and updates dependency locks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(changelog): address code review issues from Cursor Bugbot and cubic

- Fix sed multi-line prose substitution by using heredoc with variable expansion
- Fix macOS-only sed syntax to be portable across BSD and GNU sed
- Fix reversed chronological order in backfill mode by processing oldest-first
- Fix version regex to properly escape dots for literal matching

Resolves High severity issue with multi-line AI-generated prose breaking sed
Resolves Medium severity portability issues for Linux CI runners

* fix(changelog): replace mapfile with portable array loop for Bash 3.2

The mapfile builtin requires Bash 4+, which causes the --all flag to
fail on macOS (ships with Bash 3.2). Replace with a portable while
read loop that works on all Bash versions 3.2+.

Resolves cubic review comment about Bash compatibility.

* fix(changelog): address Bobby's code review on PR #751

- Fix shell injection in workflow heredoc by using quoted heredocs and
  appending AI-generated prose via echo instead of variable expansion
- Fix broken changelog link (CHANGELOG.md anchor → releases/tag URL)
- Add concurrency group to prevent overlapping workflow runs
- Add paths filter so workflow only triggers on CHANGELOG.md changes
- Remove dead code checking for individual version files never created
- Update README to reflect accordion-based single-file architecture

* fix(changelog): address follow-up review issues on PR #751

- Add missing `v` prefix in template to match duplicate detection regex
- Use awk getline instead of -v to avoid backslash corruption in prose
- Move index.md duplicate check before expensive AI generation in script
- Bind workflow dispatch inputs to env vars to prevent script injection

* fix(docs): address review comments and add v0.0.27 changelog

- Fix awk -v backslash corruption in workflow (use getline)
- Fix direct expression interpolation in workflow run blocks
- Fix unquoted heredoc shell injection in manual script
- Update README to match accordion architecture
- Add v0.0.27 release notes to changelog index

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(designer): sequential addon install and auto-enable after install (#767)

* fix(designer): sequential addon install and auto-enable after install

The server's addon install endpoint returns immediately while the actual
installation runs as a background task. When installing multiple addons,
the frontend sent all requests back-to-back, causing the second install
to hit the server's install lock and fail at 0%.

Poll each task to completion before starting the next install in both
TestChat and ManageAddons. Also auto-enable STT/TTS toggles when the
addon becomes newly installed instead of leaving them disabled.

* fix(designer): handle failed installs, dep order, and cancellation

Address PR review feedback:
- waitForTaskCompletion now throws on task failure or timeout instead
  of silently returning, so errors surface to the user
- AddonInstallSidePane orders dependencies before primaries so they
  install in the correct sequence
- Cancel button aborts the install chain via AbortController so no
  further installs start after dismissal

* fix(designer): fix error swallowing in poll loop and clear state on cancel

Move task failure check outside the network-error try/catch so failures
with any error message propagate correctly instead of being swallowed
by the string-matching catch predicate.

Clear installingAddonName on cancel so addon cards don't stay stuck in
the installing/disabled state.

* fix(designer): hide meta-addons from Manage Add-ons page

Meta-addons like "Speech Processing" exist in the registry for backend
dependency resolution but should not appear in the UI. Users install
individual addons (STT, TTS) directly with dependencies auto-resolved.

* fix(designer): re-throw HTTP errors in task polling, only retry network failures

Only swallow errors with no server response (true network hiccups).
Re-throw HTTP errors (4xx/5xx) immediately since they indicate
permanent problems like a missing task ID.

commit.message:
fix(universal-runtime): prevent event loop blocking during model loading (#743)

* fix(universal-runtime): prevent event loop blocking during model loading

Wrap synchronous transformers.from_pretrained() calls in asyncio.to_thread()
to prevent blocking the FastAPI event loop during model loading.

Previously, when an embedding or language model was being loaded (triggered
by the first request), the entire server would freeze and be unable to
respond to health checks or other requests. This caused transient E2E test
failures when multiple files were processed in parallel - the first task
would start loading the model, and subsequent tasks' health checks would
timeout.

The fix follows the same pattern already used by the GGUF model classes,
which correctly use run_in_executor() for blocking operations.

Affected files:
- runtimes/universal/models/encoder_model.py
- runtimes/universal/models/language_model.py

* fix(runtime): remove dead TYPE_CHECKING block in language_model

---------

Co-authored-by: Matt Hamann <mhamann@rownd.io>

commit.message:
fix(server): API system prompts override config system prompts (#763)

* fix(server): API system prompts override config system prompts

System messages sent via the OpenAI-compatible chat API were being added
to conversation history alongside config-defined system prompts, causing
the model to see both. Now API system prompts are extracted before
entering history and used as the sole system prompt for that request,
replacing config system prompts. Context providers (RAG) still append to
whichever system prompt is active. When no API system prompt is sent,
config system prompts continue to work as before.

* fix(server): preserve system prompt override during tool loops

Address code review findings:
- Only reset _request_system_prompt when messages are explicitly provided,
  not on messages=None (tool iteration calls). This prevents the API system
  prompt override from being lost during ChatOrchestratorAgent tool loops.
- Use isinstance check instead of str() coercion to avoid turning None
  content into the literal string "None".

commit.message:
fix(runtime): add smart GPU allocation to prevent multi-model OOM crashes (#761)

* fix(runtime): add smart GPU allocation to prevent multi-model OOM crashes

On multi-GPU systems, llama.cpp's default split_mode=LAYER distributes
every model across ALL visible devices proportionally. When a second
model loads, weaker GPUs run out of VRAM and crash the process.

This adds a GPU allocator that queries actual free VRAM per device and
routes each model to the single GPU with the most headroom. Multi-GPU
split is only used as a fallback when no single GPU can fit the model.
Also fixes context_calculator to use free VRAM instead of total memory.

* chore(runtime): address PR review feedback

- Remove unused imports (MagicMock, GPUAllocation, importlib) from tests
- Use generic error message in user-facing RuntimeError, log GPU details
  internally to avoid exposing hardware info
- Break long GPUDevice constructor lines to comply with line length rules
- Exclude GPUs with <512 MiB free from multi-GPU split to prevent OOM
  on nearly-full small devices
- Pass n_ctx=512 explicitly to encoder Llama() to match VRAM estimate
- Only recompute context size for single-GPU (split_mode=NONE), not for
  multi-GPU splits where memory is distributed across devices
- Add test for multi-GPU tiny GPU exclusion

Fixes #762

* style(runtime): apply ruff lint and format fixes

* fix(runtime): harden GPU allocator with per-device feasibility, error sanitization, and multi-GPU context sizing

- Sanitize InsufficientVRAMError to avoid leaking GPU inventory details
  to API responses; store diagnostics in gpu_details attribute for
  server-side logging
- Add per-device feasibility check to multi-GPU split path: iteratively
  prune GPUs whose free VRAM cannot cover their proportional share plus
  fixed per-device overhead (256 MiB for compute/scratch buffers)
- Re-compute context size for SPLIT_MODE_LAYER using combined free VRAM
  across participating devices instead of a single GPU's memory, which
  previously underestimated available context capacity
- Unify embedding_n_ctx constant in GGUFEncoderModel to prevent VRAM
  estimate / constructor mismatch
- Add total_free_vram to GPUAllocation and available_memory_override to
  get_default_context_size for multi-GPU memory awareness

* fix(runtime): re-allocate GPUs after multi-GPU context size adjustment

When SPLIT_MODE_LAYER context re-computation changes n_ctx, re-run
get_llama_gpu_params so tensor_split and per-device feasibility reflect
the actual KV cache size. Without this, the stale allocation computed
for the original n_ctx could OOM on a weaker GPU.

On multi-GPU systems, llama.cpp's default split_mode=LAYER distributes

This adds a GPU allocator that queries actual free VRAM per device and
routes each model to the single GPU with the most headroom. Multi-GPU
split is only used as a fallback when no single GPU can fit the model.
Also fixes context_calculator to use free VRAM instead of total memory.

* chore(runtime): address PR review feedback

- Remove unused imports (MagicMock, GPUAllocation, importlib) from tests
- Use generic error message in user-facing RuntimeError, log GPU details
  internally to avoid exposing hardware info
- Break long GPUDevice constructor lines to comply with line length rules
- Exclude GPUs with <512 MiB free from multi-GPU split to prevent OOM
  on nearly-full small devices
- Pass n_ctx=512 explicitly to encoder Llama() to match VRAM estimate
- Only recompute context size for single-GPU (split_mode=NONE), not for
  multi-GPU splits where memory is distributed across devices
- Add test for multi-GPU tiny GPU exclusion

Fixes #762

* style(runtime): apply ruff lint and format fixes

* fix(runtime): harden GPU allocator with per-device feasibility, error sanitization, and multi-GPU context sizing

- Sanitize InsufficientVRAMError to avoid leaking GPU inventory details
  to API responses; store diagnostics in gpu_details attribute for
  server-side logging
- Add per-device feasibility check to multi-GPU split path: iteratively
  prune GPUs whose free VRAM cannot cover their proportional share plus
  fixed per-device overhead (256 MiB for compute/scratch buffers)
- Re-compute context size for SPLIT_MODE_LAYER using combined free VRAM
  across participating devices instead of a single GPU's memory, which
  previously underestimated available context capacity
- Unify embedding_n_ctx constant in GGUFEncoderModel to prevent VRAM
  estimate / constructor mismatch
- Add total_free_vram to GPUAllocation and available_memory_override to
  get_default_context_size for multi-GPU memory awareness

* fix(runtime): re-allocate GPUs after multi-GPU context size adjustment

When SPLIT_MODE_LAYER context re-computation changes n_ctx, re-run
get_llama_gpu_params so tensor_split and per-device feasibility reflect
the actual KV cache size. Without this, the stale allocation computed
for the original n_ctx could OOM on a weaker GPU.

commit.message:
Feat addons UX/FED  (#758)

* feat: addons

* fix(server): remove unused variable in addon service

Remove unused result variable from subprocess.run call in install_addon_task.
The return value is not needed since check=True raises CalledProcessError on failure.

* fix(ci): install PyYAML in addon wheels build job

The build_addon_wheels.py script requires PyYAML but it was only installed
in the setup job. Add PyYAML installation to the build job as well.

* chore: trigger CI

Trigger fresh CI run to complete all addon wheel builds

* fix(addons): temporarily disable macos-x86_64 platform builds

GitHub Actions macos-13 runners are consistently being cancelled or unavailable,
preventing addon wheel builds for macos-x86_64 from completing. Temporarily
disable this platform until the runner availability issue is resolved.

* fix(ci): use dynamic matrix for addon wheels release upload

The upload-release job was using a hardcoded matrix that included all platforms,
including disabled ones like macos-x86_64. This caused the job to fail when trying
to download artifacts that were never built.

Now the upload-release job uses the same dynamic matrix from the setup job,
ensuring it only uploads artifacts for enabled platforms.

* fix(cli): bad warning during project init

* fix(ci): correct artifact download path in PyApp release upload

The download-artifact action extracts files directly without preserving
the upload path structure. Changed download path from dist/ to dist/pyapp/
so files are in the location expected by the upload step.

* fix(cli): URL-encode version in PyApp binary download URLs

The + character in version strings (e.g., v0.0.1+feat.addons) needs to be
URL-encoded as %2B for GitHub release download URLs to work properly.

Added url.PathEscape to encode the version string before constructing the
download URL.

* fix(build): binaries should honor lockfiles

* chore: always build pyapp

* fix(addons): improve path and url download handling

* fix(speech): better addons compat

* fix(audio): various speech flow improvements

* fix(runtime): move warnings.filterwarnings after imports to fix E402

The warnings.filterwarnings() call was placed between stdlib and
third-party imports, causing all subsequent imports to trigger ruff
E402 (module level import not at top of file).

* fix(runtime): replace try/except/pass with contextlib.suppress (SIM105)

* fix(runtime): update audio test mock to accept audio_array kwarg

The transcribe mock was missing the audio_array parameter that the
production code now passes for decoded audio.

* chore: pr fixes

* fix: address remaining PR review comments

- fix(runtime): use transcribe_audio_stream for numpy array streaming
  (transcribe_stream only accepts audio_path)
- fix(voice): execute all parallel tool calls instead of only the first,
  ensuring every tool_call gets a matching tool result per OpenAI contract
- fix(voice): stash non-matching tool results by ID instead of re-queuing
  to avoid tight spin when queue is non-empty
- fix(server): run blocking subprocess.run calls via asyncio.to_thread
  in addon install/restart to avoid stalling the event loop
- fix(cli): add O_TRUNC flag to extractTarGz and check Close() errors
  on writable file handles in both tar and zip extraction
- fix(cli): export HardwareNotesRaw field so yaml.Unmarshal can populate
  hardware_notes from addon registry YAML files
- fix(cli): track whether PYTHONPATH was explicitly set in service env
  so an intentionally empty value isn't overridden by os.Getenv fallback
- fix(shims): preserve PYTHONPATH entry order when injecting into sys.path
  (insert at incrementing index instead of always index 0)
- fix(tools): reject unknown Linux architectures in get_host_platform
  instead of silently defaulting to x86_64
- fix(ci): sanitize newlines from branch input before writing to
  GITHUB_ENV to prevent environment variable injection
- fix(runtime): add debug logging to empty except in GGUF metadata cache
- fix(docs): wrap top-level await in asyncio.run() in API docs example

* fix: address follow-up review comments

- fix(shims): track actual insert position for PYTHONPATH entries so
  skipped duplicates don't shift later entries to wrong positions
- fix(voice): emit error results for remaining tool calls when
  interrupted mid-loop, ensuring every tool_call in the assistant
  message has a matching tool result per OpenAI contract

* fix: address third-round PR review comments

- fix(cli): map Go arm64 to aarch64 for Linux in getPlatformString
- fix(cli): use strings.Join for search path error message
- fix(cli): add global lock to addon uninstall to prevent races
- fix(server): make uninstall_addon async with asyncio.to_thread
- fix(ci): add workflow_call trigger to build-addon-wheels.yml
- fix(ci): auto-discover addons from registry in matrix generation
- fix(ci): add onnxruntime to addon choices and build matrices

* fix(ci): dynamically generate snapshot upload matrix from platforms.yaml

The upload-to-snapshot job hardcoded all 5 platforms including disabled
ones (windows-x86_64, macos-x86_64), causing artifact download failures
since build-addon-wheels only builds enabled platforms. Replace the
hardcoded matrix with a dynamic setup job using generate_platform_matrix.py
which reads from platforms.yaml and the addon registry.

* fix(ci): use auto-discovery for addon build matrix instead of hardcoded list

The release/PR/workflow_call path hardcoded ADDONS="stt,tts", excluding
onnxruntime which stt depends on. Now omits --addons entirely for these
cases so generate_platform_matrix.py auto-discovers all addons with
packages from the registry directory.

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* feat(designer): inline addon installation UX for Speech features

Implements contextual, inline add-on installation experience for Speech
features (STT, TTS) in the Designer. Users can now discover and install
add-ons directly within the Test page without leaving their workflow.

**Frontend (Designer)**
- Add API service layer for addon endpoints (addonsService.ts)
- Add TypeScript types matching backend Pydantic models (addons.ts)
- Add TanStack Query hooks with smart polling (useAddons.ts)
- Add installation side panel with addon details (AddonInstallSidePane.tsx)
- Add progress tracking panel with minimize capability (AddonInstallProgress.tsx)
- Add minimized progress indicator (AddonInstallMinimized.tsx)
- Integrate empty state in Test page Speech mode
- Add conditional rendering based on addon installation status

**Backend (Server)**
- Add restart script for universal-runtime service (restart_runtime.sh)
- Integrate restart script into addon installation flow
- Use subprocess.Popen for non-blocking service restart

**Runtime**
- Add faster-whisper and av packages back to base dependencies
- Enable Speech functionality when installed via addon system

**User Experience**
1. User selects Speech mode → sees empty state with "Add on" button
2. Click button → side panel shows addon details (STT, TTS, sizes)
3. User confirms → installation starts with progress tracking
4. User can minimize progress and continue working
5. Services restart automatically (~30-60s downtime)
6. Speech features become available after restart

Backend state tracked in ~/.llamafarm/addons.json (not server/addon_installation_state.json).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(designer): comprehensive add-ons management UX

Implements full add-ons discovery and management experience:

**Home page:**
- New "Add-ons" section showing 3 installed add-ons
- Empty slots with "Browse add-ons" placeholders (clickable, navigate to /addons)
- Reduced spacing (mt-12 lg:mt-16 instead of mt-20 lg:mt-28)
- Click installed addon → navigate to manage page
- Click uninstalled addon → open install panel

**Manage Add-ons page (/addons):**
- Split view: "My add-ons" (installed) and "Available add-ons" (not installed)
- Search filters both sections simultaneously
- Hide composite/meta add-ons when all dependencies installed (e.g., "Speech Processing")
- Installing state: cards show "Installing..." badge with pulse animation, buttons disabled
- Version badges on installed add-ons (e.g., "v1.0.0")

**Dependency management:**
- Dependency warning modal with 3 options:
  1. Remove all (cascade uninstall: addon + dependents)
  2. Remove only [name] (leaves dependents broken)
  3. Cancel
- Backend auto-installs dependencies recursively
- UI bundles dependencies in install confirmation panel

**UX improvements:**
- No slide-in animations on modals (instant display)
- Proper modal for removal confirmation (not browser confirm)
- Better error messages: "Failed to install Speech-to-Text. Please try again."
- Toast shows cascade count: "Removed ONNX Runtime and 2 dependent add-ons"
- Add-ons move between sections after state changes (no refresh needed)

**TODO for backend:**
- Expose package count and calculate actual size (currently hardcoded ~200MB)
- Expose hardware_notes from YAML (CUDA/CPU/Metal support)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(addons): backend dependency auto-installation and generic UI components

**Backend - Recursive dependency installation:**
- Added `_install_dependencies()` to recursively install addon dependencies
- Added `_install_single_addon()` to install one addon without dependencies
- Modified `install_addon_task()` to auto-install dependencies
- Fixes bug where installing STT didn't auto-install onnxruntime
- Prevents circular dependencies with `installing_chain` set

**Designer - Generic addon components:**
- Made `AddonInstallSidePane` generic (not hardcoded to STT/TTS)
- Auto-detects primary vs dependency add-ons dynamically
- Works for any add-on, enabling future extensibility

**Designer - Installation UX improvements:**
- Added 2-second delay after completion so users see success state
- Fixed query invalidation timing (moved to onComplete callback)
- Updated Test page to use new generic side panel
- Proper cache invalidation after installation completes

These changes enable the dependency bundling and auto-installation features
used by the Manage Add-ons page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(server): ignore addon installation state file

Runtime state file should not be committed (similar to cache/session files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(designer): require STT addon for microphone in text generation

Add STT availability check to microphone button in text generation.
When user clicks mic without STT installed, shows addon installation
modal instead of attempting to record.

- Fetch addon list in both speech and inference modes
- Check STT installation before starting voice recording
- Open addon installation side pane if STT not available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* feat(designer): add contextual add-ons link to speech config

Add small package icon next to STT/TTS toggles that links to add-ons
management page. Provides contextual access without cluttering header.

- Create reusable AddonsLink component with tooltip
- Add to SpeechToTextConfig and TextToSpeechConfig
- Tooltip: "This feature is an add-on. Click to manage add-ons"
- Subtle styling (muted color, hover effect)
- Keyboard accessible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): move add-ons icon beside title instead of toggle

Reposition package icon to appear right beside the feature title
(Speech-to-Text/Text-to-Speech) rather than after the toggle.
This keeps the toggle in its original position and makes the icon
more contextually associated with the feature name.

Layout: [Title 📦] ... [Toggle Status]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(runtime): remove STT dependencies from base runtime

Remove faster-whisper and av from base dependencies since STT is
now an optional addon. These packages are installed when users
install the STT addon via the Designer.

This completes the transition to addon-based architecture for
speech features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): fix lock usage, remove redundant restarts, and deduplicate PYTHONPATH logic

- Use shared lock (TryRLockContext) for reading addon state to allow concurrent reads
- Remove redundant service restart in Python addon service (CLI already handles it)
- Extract duplicated PYTHONPATH injection logic into helper method

These changes improve addon installation reliability and reduce unnecessary
service downtime during addon operations.

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): improve error handling, service management, and cross-platform support

- Use shutil.which() for cross-platform binary lookup instead of Unix-specific subprocess
- Add URL package spec handling to extract package names from wheel URLs
- Add keepPackages checks before removing metadata to prevent breaking kept packages
- Handle file close errors properly to prevent silent data loss
- Add deferred service restart on install/uninstall failures to prevent services being left stopped
- Read CI platform config from addons/platforms.yaml (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding
- Update workflow dropdowns to only show enabled platforms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): fix addon installation by clearing VIRTUAL_ENV

Fixed critical bug where addon packages weren't being installed despite
successful API responses. The issue was that the server process inherited
VIRTUAL_ENV environment variable pointing to the wrong Python environment,
causing `uv add` to target the wrong venv.

**Root Cause:**
- Server runs with VIRTUAL_ENV set to system Python
- `uv add` respects VIRTUAL_ENV and tries to add packages there
- But the universal runtime uses its own `.venv` in runtimes/universal/
- Result: packages were never installed in the correct environment

**Fix:**
- Clear VIRTUAL_ENV before running `uv add` and `uv remove`
- Added logging of stdout/stderr from uv commands for debugging
- Both installation and uninstallation now work correctly

**Additional Changes:**
- Removed speech.yaml meta-addon per user request (users choose STT/TTS directly)
- Updated tts.yaml to depend on onnxruntime addon
- Added comprehensive test script (test_addons.sh) that verifies:
  - Addon installation via API
  - Packages actually installed in runtime venv
  - State file correctly updated
  - Server restart works
  - Addon removal fully removes packages
  - Dependency handling (onnxruntime)

**Test Results:**
All 13 tests pass:
✓ Addon installation API works
✓ Packages installed in correct venv
✓ State file updated correctly
✓ Services restart after installation
✓ STT endpoint responds
✓ Addon removal works
✓ Packages fully removed
✓ Dependencies handled correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(server): improve error handling for gated HuggingFace models

Detect when a model requires HuggingFace authentication and provide
a clear, actionable error message instead of showing the raw API error.

Changes:
- Add detection for "gated repo" and "401 client error" messages
- Return HTTP 401 with user-friendly message suggesting authentication
  or model change
- Include model name in error message for better context

Also includes addon package installations in universal-runtime that were
added during addon testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): address code review issues for addon UX

Frontend fixes from code review:
- Add explicit type="button" to AddonsLink to prevent form submission
- Guard install prompt in TestChat until addons list loads
- Sync sttEnabled/ttsEnabled with sttInstalled/ttsInstalled in SpeechTestPanel
  to prevent attempting to use uninstalled addons
- Fix AddonInstallSidePane to only show dependencies for selected addons,
  not all dependencies

These changes improve addon UX by preventing edge cases where:
- Button clicks trigger unwanted form submissions
- Loading states cause premature install prompts
- Users can try to use features without required addons installed
- Dependency list shows irrelevant addons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): addon appears in available list after removal

**Problem:** When removing an addon (e.g., TTS), it would not appear in the "Available add-ons" section if its dependencies were still installed (e.g., onnxruntime).

**Root Cause:** The filtering logic in ManageAddons was hiding ALL addons with satisfied dependencies, not just meta/composite addons (addons with no packages that only bundle dependencies).

**Solution:**
- Added `packages` field to `AddonInfo` type (backend + frontend)
- Updated filtering logic to only hide meta addons (where packages.length === 0)
- Regular addons now appear in "Available add-ons" immediately after removal

**Changes:**
- server/api/routers/addons/types.py: Add packages field to AddonInfo
- server/api/routers/addons/service.py: Populate packages field, remove unused variable
- designer/src/types/addons.ts: Add packages field to AddonInfo interface
- designer/src/pages/ManageAddons.tsx: Fix filtering logic to check for meta addons
- runtimes/universal/pyproject.toml: Clean up test packages from main dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): remove CLI call from addon installation

**Problem:** Installing addons failed with "unknown command 'addons' for 'lf'" error.

**Root Cause:** The merged feat-addons code called `lf addons install <addon_name>`, but the CLI doesn't have this command implemented yet.

**Solution:** Remove the CLI call and handle installation entirely in the service. The service already:
- Installs packages via `uv add`
- Marks addon as installed in state file
- Returns completion status to UI

User can manually restart universal-runtime after installation to use the new addon.

**Changes:**
- server/api/routers/addons/service.py: Remove CLI subprocess call
- Updated completion message to inform user to restart service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): add uv pip fallback for addon install and improve error handling

CLI-based addon install downloads pre-built wheels from GitHub releases,
which return 404 in development. Add a fallback that installs packages
directly via uv pip install from PyPI with full dependency resolution.

- Try CLI wheel install first, fall back to uv pip on failure
- Resolve addon dependency chains (topological sort with cycle detection)
- Track install_method in addons.json so uninstall uses matching strategy
- Add error handling to uninstall endpoint (ValueError, FileNotFoundError, RuntimeError)
- Prefer dist/lf binary for development builds (finds addon registry)
- Extract restart logic into reusable _restart_component method

* fix: address PR review issues across addon system

- Fix P1 bug: _restart_component terminal status was overwritten by
  caller's unconditional "Installation complete" message. Now returns
  bool to signal caller to skip its final status update.
- Sanitize error responses in uninstall endpoint to avoid leaking
  internal paths from FileNotFoundError/RuntimeError.
- Remove useless ternary in AddonInstallSidePane (open is always true
  after early return guard).
- Compute full transitive dependency closure in side pane so all
  indirect deps (e.g. onnxruntime via stt) are shown.
- Fix hardcoded absolute path in test_addons.sh; derive from script
  location instead.

* fix(server): address remaining PR #758 review comments

- Fix native audio tool call loop to execute all tool calls, not just
  the first (P1 protocol violation - OpenAI requires result for every
  tool_call in assistant message)
- Add install_lock to prevent concurrent addon install/uninstall races
- Use Field(default_factory=list) for mutable defaults in AddonInfo
- Replace hardcoded path in restart_runtime.sh with script-relative
- Tighten spaCy version to >=3.8.0,<3.9.0 to match en_core_web_sm-3.8.0

---------

Co-authored-by: Matt Hamann <mhamann@rownd.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

**Home page:**

- Empty slots with "Browse add-ons" placeholders (clickable, navigate to /addons)
- Reduced spacing (mt-12 lg:mt-16 instead of mt-20 lg:mt-28)
- Click installed addon → navigate to manage page
- Click uninstalled addon → open install panel

**Manage Add-ons page (/addons):**
- Split view: "My add-ons" (installed) and "Available add-ons" (not installed)
- Search filters both sections simultaneously
- Hide composite/meta add-ons when all dependencies installed (e.g., "Speech Processing")
- Installing state: cards show "Installing..." badge with pulse animation, buttons disabled
- Version badges on installed add-ons (e.g., "v1.0.0")

**Dependency management:**
- Dependency warning modal with 3 options:
  1. Remove all (cascade uninstall: addon + dependents)
  2. Remove only [name] (leaves dependents broken)
  3. Cancel
- Backend auto-installs dependencies recursively
- UI bundles dependencies in install confirmation panel

**UX improvements:**
- No slide-in animations on modals (instant display)
- Proper modal for removal confirmation (not browser confirm)
- Better error messages: "Failed to install Speech-to-Text. Please try again."
- Toast shows cascade count: "Removed ONNX Runtime and 2 dependent add-ons"
- Add-ons move between sections after state changes (no refresh needed)

**TODO for backend:**
- Expose package count and calculate actual size (currently hardcoded ~200MB)
- Expose hardware_notes from YAML (CUDA/CPU/Metal support)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(addons): backend dependency auto-installation and generic UI components

**Backend - Recursive dependency installation:**
- Added `_install_dependencies()` to recursively install addon dependencies
- Added `_install_single_addon()` to install one addon without dependencies
- Modified `install_addon_task()` to auto-install dependencies
- Fixes bug where installing STT didn't auto-install onnxruntime
- Prevents circular dependencies with `installing_chain` set

**Designer - Generic addon components:**
- Made `AddonInstallSidePane` generic (not hardcoded to STT/TTS)
- Auto-detects primary vs dependency add-ons dynamically
- Works for any add-on, enabling future extensibility

**Designer - Installation UX improvements:**
- Added 2-second delay after completion so users see success state
- Fixed query invalidation timing (moved to onComplete callback)
- Updated Test page to use new generic side panel
- Proper cache invalidation after installation completes

These changes enable the dependency bundling and auto-installation features
used by the Manage Add-ons page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(server): ignore addon installation state file

Runtime state file should not be committed (similar to cache/session files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(designer): require STT addon for microphone in text generation

Add STT availability check to microphone button in text generation.
When user clicks mic without STT installed, shows addon installation
modal instead of attempting to record.

- Fetch addon list in both speech and inference modes
- Check STT installation before starting voice recording
- Open addon installation side pane if STT not available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* feat(designer): add contextual add-ons link to speech config

Add small package icon next to STT/TTS toggles that links to add-ons
management page. Provides contextual access without cluttering header.

- Create reusable AddonsLink component with tooltip
- Add to SpeechToTextConfig and TextToSpeechConfig
- Tooltip: "This feature is an add-on. Click to manage add-ons"
- Subtle styling (muted color, hover effect)
- Keyboard accessible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): move add-ons icon beside title instead of toggle

Reposition package icon to appear right beside the feature title
(Speech-to-Text/Text-to-Speech) rather than after the toggle.
This keeps the toggle in its original position and makes the icon
more contextually associated with the feature name.

Layout: [Title 📦] ... [Toggle Status]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(runtime): remove STT dependencies from base runtime

Remove faster-whisper and av from base dependencies since STT is
now an optional addon. These packages are installed when users
install the STT addon via the Designer.

This completes the transition to addon-based architecture for
speech features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): fix lock usage, remove redundant restarts, and deduplicate PYTHONPATH logic

- Use shared lock (TryRLockContext) for reading addon state to allow concurrent reads
- Remove redundant service restart in Python addon service (CLI already handles it)
- Extract duplicated PYTHONPATH injection logic into helper method

These changes improve addon installation reliability and reduce unnecessary
service downtime during addon operations.

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): improve error handling, service management, and cross-platform support

- Use shutil.which() for cross-platform binary lookup instead of Unix-specific subprocess
- Add URL package spec handling to extract package names from wheel URLs
- Add keepPackages checks before removing metadata to prevent breaking kept packages
- Handle file close errors properly to prevent silent data loss
- Add deferred service restart on install/uninstall failures to prevent services being left stopped
- Read CI platform config from addons/platforms.yaml (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding
- Update workflow dropdowns to only show enabled platforms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): fix addon installation by clearing VIRTUAL_ENV

Fixed critical bug where addon packages weren't being installed despite
successful API responses. The issue was that the server process inherited
VIRTUAL_ENV environment variable pointing to the wrong Python environment,
causing `uv add` to target the wrong venv.

**Root Cause:**
- Server runs with VIRTUAL_ENV set to system Python
- `uv add` respects VIRTUAL_ENV and tries to add packages there
- But the universal runtime uses its own `.venv` in runtimes/universal/
- Result: packages were never installed in the correct environment

**Fix:**
- Clear VIRTUAL_ENV before running `uv add` and `uv remove`
- Added logging of stdout/stderr from uv commands for debugging
- Both installation and uninstallation now work correctly

**Additional Changes:**
- Removed speech.yaml meta-addon per user request (users choose STT/TTS directly)
- Updated tts.yaml to depend on onnxruntime addon
- Added comprehensive test script (test_addons.sh) that verifies:
  - Addon installation via API
  - Packages actually installed in runtime venv
  - State file correctly updated
  - Server restart works
  - Addon removal fully removes packages
  - Dependency handling (onnxruntime)

**Test Results:**
All 13 tests pass:
✓ Addon installation API works
✓ Packages installed in correct venv
✓ State file updated correctly
✓ Services restart after installation
✓ STT endpoint responds
✓ Addon removal works
✓ Packages fully removed
✓ Dependencies handled correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(server): improve error handling for gated HuggingFace models

Detect when a model requires HuggingFace authentication and provide
a clear, actionable error message instead of showing the raw API error.

Changes:
- Add detection for "gated repo" and "401 client error" messages
- Return HTTP 401 with user-friendly message suggesting authentication
  or model change
- Include model name in error message for better context

Also includes addon package installations in universal-runtime that were
added during addon testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): address code review issues for addon UX

Frontend fixes from code review:
- Add explicit type="button" to AddonsLink to prevent form submission
- Guard install prompt in TestChat until addons list loads
- Sync sttEnabled/ttsEnabled with sttInstalled/ttsInstalled in SpeechTestPanel
  to prevent attempting to use uninstalled addons
- Fix AddonInstallSidePane to only show dependencies for selected addons,
  not all dependencies

These changes improve addon UX by preventing edge cases where:
- Button clicks trigger unwanted form submissions
- Loading states cause premature install prompts
- Users can try to use features without required addons installed
- Dependency list shows irrelevant addons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(designer): addon appears in available list after removal

**Problem:** When removing an addon (e.g., TTS), it would not appear in the "Available add-ons" section if its dependencies were still installed (e.g., onnxruntime).

**Root Cause:** The filtering logic in ManageAddons was hiding ALL addons with satisfied dependencies, not just meta/composite addons (addons with no packages that only bundle dependencies).

**Solution:**
- Added `packages` field to `AddonInfo` type (backend + frontend)
- Updated filtering logic to only hide meta addons (where packages.length === 0)
- Regular addons now appear in "Available add-ons" immediately after removal

**Changes:**
- server/api/routers/addons/types.py: Add packages field to AddonInfo
- server/api/routers/addons/service.py: Populate packages field, remove unused variable
- designer/src/types/addons.ts: Add packages field to AddonInfo interface
- designer/src/pages/ManageAddons.tsx: Fix filtering logic to check for meta addons
- runtimes/universal/pyproject.toml: Clean up test packages from main dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): remove CLI call from addon installation

**Problem:** Installing addons failed with "unknown command 'addons' for 'lf'" error.

**Root Cause:** The merged feat-addons code called `lf addons install <addon_name>`, but the CLI doesn't have this command implemented yet.

**Solution:** Remove the CLI call and handle installation entirely in the service. The service already:
- Installs packages via `uv add`
- Marks addon as installed in state file
- Returns completion status to UI

User can manually restart universal-runtime after installation to use the new addon.

**Changes:**
- server/api/routers/addons/service.py: Remove CLI subprocess call
- Updated completion message to inform user to restart service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): add uv pip fallback for addon install and improve error handling

CLI-based addon install downloads pre-built wheels from GitHub releases,
which return 404 in development. Add a fallback that installs packages
directly via uv pip install from PyPI with full dependency resolution.

- Try CLI wheel install first, fall back to uv pip on failure
- Resolve addon dependency chains (topological sort with cycle detection)
- Track install_method in addons.json so uninstall uses matching strategy
- Add error handling to uninstall endpoint (ValueError, FileNotFoundError, RuntimeError)
- Prefer dist/lf binary for development builds (finds addon registry)
- Extract restart logic into reusable _restart_component method

* fix: address PR review issues across addon system

- Fix P1 bug: _restart_component terminal status was overwritten by
  caller's unconditional "Installation complete" message. Now returns
  bool to signal caller to skip its final status update.
- Sanitize error responses in uninstall endpoint to avoid leaking
  internal paths from FileNotFoundError/RuntimeError.
- Remove useless ternary in AddonInstallSidePane (open is always true
  after early return guard).
- Compute full transitive dependency closure in side pane so all
  indirect deps (e.g. onnxruntime via stt) are shown.
- Fix hardcoded absolute path in test_addons.sh; derive from script
  location instead.

* fix(server): address remaining PR #758 review comments

- Fix native audio tool call loop to execute all tool calls, not just
  the first (P1 protocol violation - OpenAI requires result for every
  tool_call in assistant message)
- Add install_lock to prevent concurrent addon install/uninstall races
- Use Field(default_factory=list) for mutable defaults in AddonInfo
- Replace hardcoded path in restart_runtime.sh with script-relative
- Tighten spaCy version to >=3.8.0,<3.9.0 to match en_core_web_sm-3.8.0

---------

Co-authored-by: Matt Hamann <mhamann@rownd.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(config): add per-model RAG defaults (#759)

* feat(config): add per-model RAG defaults (rag_enabled, target_database)

Allow each model in the config to specify default RAG behavior and
target database. These optional fields cascade with existing resolution:
request params > model config > project config > hardcoded fallbacks.
Includes tests and documentation.

* fix(server): pass model_config through RAG search chain

Address Qodo review findings:
- Pass model_config to _perform_rag_search_and_add_to_context so
  target_database is used in actual retrieval, not just logging
- Shorten long lines to comply with 88-char line limit
- Shorten test docstrings for Ruff compliance

commit.message:
feat: addons (#748)

* feat: addons

* fix(server): remove unused variable in addon service

Remove unused result variable from subprocess.run call in install_addon_task.
The return value is not needed since check=True raises CalledProcessError on failure.

* fix(ci): install PyYAML in addon wheels build job

The build_addon_wheels.py script requires PyYAML but it was only installed
in the setup job. Add PyYAML installation to the build job as well.

* chore: trigger CI

Trigger fresh CI run to complete all addon wheel builds

* fix(addons): temporarily disable macos-x86_64 platform builds

GitHub Actions macos-13 runners are consistently being cancelled or unavailable,
preventing addon wheel builds for macos-x86_64 from completing. Temporarily
disable this platform until the runner availability issue is resolved.

* fix(ci): use dynamic matrix for addon wheels release upload

The upload-release job was using a hardcoded matrix that included all platforms,
including disabled ones like macos-x86_64. This caused the job to fail when trying
to download artifacts that were never built.

Now the upload-release job uses the same dynamic matrix from the setup job,
ensuring it only uploads artifacts for enabled platforms.

* fix(cli): bad warning during project init

* fix(ci): correct artifact download path in PyApp release upload

The download-artifact action extracts files directly without preserving
the upload path structure. Changed download path from dist/ to dist/pyapp/
so files are in the location expected by the upload step.

* fix(cli): URL-encode version in PyApp binary download URLs

The + character in version strings (e.g., v0.0.1+feat.addons) needs to be
URL-encoded as %2B for GitHub release download URLs to work properly.

Added url.PathEscape to encode the version string before constructing the
download URL.

* fix(build): binaries should honor lockfiles

* chore: always build pyapp

* fix(addons): improve path and url download handling

* fix(speech): better addons compat

* fix(audio): various speech flow improvements

* fix(runtime): move warnings.filterwarnings after imports to fix E402

The warnings.filterwarnings() call was placed between stdlib and
third-party imports, causing all subsequent imports to trigger ruff
E402 (module level import not at top of file).

* fix(runtime): replace try/except/pass with contextlib.suppress (SIM105)

* fix(runtime): update audio test mock to accept audio_array kwarg

The transcribe mock was missing the audio_array parameter that the
production code now passes for decoded audio.

* chore: pr fixes

* fix: address remaining PR review comments

- fix(runtime): use transcribe_audio_stream for numpy array streaming
  (transcribe_stream only accepts audio_path)
- fix(voice): execute all parallel tool calls instead of only the first,
  ensuring every tool_call gets a matching tool result per OpenAI contract
- fix(voice): stash non-matching tool results by ID instead of re-queuing
  to avoid tight spin when queue is non-empty
- fix(server): run blocking subprocess.run calls via asyncio.to_thread
  in addon install/restart to avoid stalling the event loop
- fix(cli): add O_TRUNC flag to extractTarGz and check Close() errors
  on writable file handles in both tar and zip extraction
- fix(cli): export HardwareNotesRaw field so yaml.Unmarshal can populate
  hardware_notes from addon registry YAML files
- fix(cli): track whether PYTHONPATH was explicitly set in service env
  so an intentionally empty value isn't overridden by os.Getenv fallback
- fix(shims): preserve PYTHONPATH entry order when injecting into sys.path
  (insert at incrementing index instead of always index 0)
- fix(tools): reject unknown Linux architectures in get_host_platform
  instead of silently defaulting to x86_64
- fix(ci): sanitize newlines from branch input before writing to
  GITHUB_ENV to prevent environment variable injection
- fix(runtime): add debug logging to empty except in GGUF metadata cache
- fix(docs): wrap top-level await in asyncio.run() in API docs example

* fix: address follow-up review comments

- fix(shims): track actual insert position for PYTHONPATH entries so
  skipped duplicates don't shift later entries to wrong positions
- fix(voice): emit error results for remaining tool calls when
  interrupted mid-loop, ensuring every tool_call in the assistant
  message has a matching tool result per OpenAI contract

* fix: address third-round PR review comments

- fix(cli): map Go arm64 to aarch64 for Linux in getPlatformString
- fix(cli): use strings.Join for search path error message
- fix(cli): add global lock to addon uninstall to prevent races
- fix(server): make uninstall_addon async with asyncio.to_thread
- fix(ci): add workflow_call trigger to build-addon-wheels.yml
- fix(ci): auto-discover addons from registry in matrix generation
- fix(ci): add onnxruntime to addon choices and build matrices

* fix(ci): dynamically generate snapshot upload matrix from platforms.yaml

The upload-to-snapshot job hardcoded all 5 platforms including disabled
ones (windows-x86_64, macos-x86_64), causing artifact download failures
since build-addon-wheels only builds enabled platforms. Replace the
hardcoded matrix with a dynamic setup job using generate_platform_matrix.py
which reads from platforms.yaml and the addon registry.

* fix(ci): use auto-discovery for addon build matrix instead of hardcoded list

The release/PR/workflow_call path hardcoded ADDONS="stt,tts", excluding
onnxruntime which stt depends on. Now omits --addons entirely for these
cases so generate_platform_matrix.py auto-discovers all addons with
packages from the registry directory.

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): fix lock usage, remove redundant restarts, and deduplicate PYTHONPATH logic

- Use shared lock (TryRLockContext) for reading addon state to allow concurrent reads
- Remove redundant service restart in Python addon service (CLI already handles it)
- Extract duplicated PYTHONPATH injection logic into helper method

These changes improve addon installation reliability and reduce unnecessary
service downtime during addon operations.

* chore: pr fixes

* fix(addons): improve error handling, service management, and cross-platform support

- Use shutil.which() for cross-platform binary lookup instead of Unix-specific subprocess
- Add URL package spec handling to extract package names from wheel URLs
- Add keepPackages checks before removing metadata to prevent breaking kept packages
- Handle file close errors properly to prevent silent data loss
- Add deferred service restart on install/uninstall failures to prevent services being left stopped
- Read CI platform config from addons/platforms.yaml (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding
- Update workflow dropdowns to only show enabled platforms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

* chore: pr fixes

- Replace hardcoded addon denylist with dynamic venv introspection
- Add keep_packages YAML field for explicit transitive dep retention
- Convert addon registry from sync.Once global to instance-based pattern
- Add --no-restart flag to CLI install/uninstall commands
- Rewrite server addon service: --no-restart, output surfacing, disk-persisted task status, component-only restart
- Add reload_addon_registry() for cache invalidation after install/uninstall
- Validate LF_ADDON_REPO_OWNER/NAME/RELEASE_TAG env vars against injection
- Clarify LIFO defer ordering in downloader temp file cleanup
- Move gofrs/flock from indirect to direct dependency
- Scope build-llama.yml contents:write to job level (least privilege)
- Consolidate task status access behind async lock, remove lockless sync path
- Narrow WebSocket suppress(Exception) to suppress(WebSocketDisconnect, RuntimeError)
- Remove no-op finally:pass block
- Fix _FALLBACK_BYTES_PER_TOKEN from 256 bytes to 256 KB to prevent OOM

* chore: pr fixes

- Run ruff autofix and resolve remaining lint issues
- Normalize package dir names in addon cleanup to match map keys
- Prune persisted task files older than 24h during recovery
- Auto-discover addons in test-addon-branch workflow instead of hardcoding

* chore: pr fixups

Co-authored-by: cubic-dev-ai[bot] <191113872+cubic-dev-ai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: cubic-dev-ai[bot] <191113872+cubic-dev-ai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add --no-restart flag to CLI install/uninstall commands
- Rewrite server addon service: --no-restart, output surfacing, disk-persisted task status, component-only restart
- Add reload_addon_registry() for cache invalidation after install/uninstall
- Validate LF_ADDON_REPO_OWNER/NAME/RELEASE_TAG env vars against injection
- Clarify LIFO defer ordering in downloader temp file cleanup
- Move gofrs/flock from indirect to direct dependency
- Scope build-llama.yml contents:write to job level (least privilege)
- Consolidate task status access behind async lock, remove lockless sync path
- Narrow WebSocket suppress(Exception) to suppress(WebSocketDisconnect, RuntimeError)
- Remove no-op finally:pass block
- Fix _FALLBACK_BYTES_PER_TOKEN from 256 bytes to 256 KB to prevent OOM

* chore: pr fixes

- Run ruff autofix and resolve remaining lint issues
- Normalize package dir names in addon cleanup to match map keys
- Prune persisted task files older than 24h during recovery
- Auto-discover addons in test-addon-branch workflow instead of hardcoding

* chore: pr fixups

commit.message:
show References accordion when RAG finds no chunks (#749)

* fix(designer): show References accordion when RAG finds no chunks

Previously, the References accordion only appeared when RAG returned
actual chunks. Now it shows "References (0)" when RAG is enabled but
finds nothing, distinguishing this from when RAG is disabled entirely.

Changes:
- Server always yields sources event (even empty) when include_sources=true
- Client initializes sources as undefined instead of empty array
- projectSessionManager preserves empty arrays in message sources

* fix(designer): respect showReferences setting for References accordion

The References accordion now only appears when showReferences is enabled,
fixing a regression where it would show for any message with sources.

* refactor(server): address PR review feedback

- Remove debug log from response_utils.py
- Move _first_not_none helper to module scope for better performance

Addresses feedback from PR #749

* refactor(server): remove debug log from sources event

Removed info-level log that was firing on every RAG-enabled request.
This log was flagged by reviewer as adding unnecessary noise to production logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
