commit.message:
fix(cli): prevent dev builds from stopping running services (#699)

Dev builds incorrectly stopped all running servers when executing CLI
commands that trigger the orchestrator. This happened because
GetCurrentCLIVersion() returns "main" for dev builds, but the check
was comparing against "dev".

Fix by checking buildinfo.CurrentVersion directly, which correctly
identifies dev builds and skips source management entirely.

Fixes #698

fix(cli): prevent dev builds from stopping running services (#699)

Dev builds incorrectly stopped all running servers when executing CLI
commands that trigger the orchestrator. This happened because
GetCurrentCLIVersion() returns "main" for dev builds, but the check
was comparing against "dev".

Fix by checking buildinfo.CurrentVersion directly, which correctly
identifies dev builds and skips source management entirely.

Fixes #698

commit.message:
chore(scripts): add wt worktree manager for parallel development (#700)

* feat(scripts): add wt worktree manager for parallel development

Adds a comprehensive worktree management tool for running multiple
LlamaFarm development environments in parallel without port conflicts.

Key features:
- Automatic port allocation with collision detection
- Service lifecycle management (start/stop/restart)
- `wt init` command for shell setup (bash/zsh/fish)
- Tab completions for all shells
- Caddy reverse proxy support for port-free URLs
- VS Code/Cursor workspace configuration

The `wt init` pattern follows industry standards (rbenv, direnv) where
shell initialization code is generated dynamically, ensuring a single
source of truth for the shell function and completions.

* chore: remove WTPLAN.md

* feat(skills): add wt worktree manager skill

Adds user-invocable /wt skill for Claude Code to manage LlamaFarm
worktrees. Includes quick reference, common workflows, and links
to full documentation.

* fix(scripts): fix wt health endpoint and RAG worker celery config

- Fix health endpoint path in cmd_health() from /v1/health to /health
- Pass Celery environment variables explicitly to RAG worker subprocess

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scripts): fix wt test list check pipe buffering issue

Capture list output to variable before grepping to avoid race
condition where grep -q exits early and causes SIGPIPE.

chore(scripts): add wt worktree manager for parallel development (#700)

* feat(scripts): add wt worktree manager for parallel development

Adds a comprehensive worktree management tool for running multiple
LlamaFarm development environments in parallel without port conflicts.

Key features:
- Automatic port allocation with collision detection
- Service lifecycle management (start/stop/restart)
- `wt init` command for shell setup (bash/zsh/fish)
- Tab completions for all shells
- Caddy reverse proxy support for port-free URLs
- VS Code/Cursor workspace configuration

The `wt init` pattern follows industry standards (rbenv, direnv) where
shell initialization code is generated dynamically, ensuring a single
source of truth for the shell function and completions.

* chore: remove WTPLAN.md

* feat(skills): add wt worktree manager skill

Adds user-invocable /wt skill for Claude Code to manage LlamaFarm
worktrees. Includes quick reference, common workflows, and links
to full documentation.

* fix(scripts): fix wt health endpoint and RAG worker celery config

- Fix health endpoint path in cmd_health() from /v1/health to /health
- Pass Celery environment variables explicitly to RAG worker subprocess

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scripts): fix wt test list check pipe buffering issue

Capture list output to variable before grepping to avoid race
condition where grep -q exits early and causes SIGPIPE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Designer onboarding flow (#691)

* feat(designer): add onboarding wizard and checklist flow

Add a 5-step onboarding wizard that collects user preferences (project type,
data status, deploy target, experience level) and generates a personalized
getting started checklist. Features include:

- Project-specific onboarding state persisted to localStorage
- Full-width horizontal checklist displayed at top of dashboard
- Playful card design with emojis, gradients, and animations
- Direct navigation links to specific pages (e.g., model training)
- Skip/restart capability with banner prompt
- Automatic wizard launch for new/empty projects

* feat(designer): enhance onboarding wizard UX and sample data import

- Add inline sample dataset picker when "sample data" option selected
- Auto-import sample dataset on wizard completion via custom events
- Fix processMutation reference error in Data.tsx auto-import
- Replace emojis with Lucide icons in wizard selector cards
- Improve dark mode accessibility for icon colors
- Add CollapsedChecklist component for dismissed checklist state
- Update checkbox dark mode border visibility
- Make navigation buttons more prominent (larger, outlined)
- Add confetti celebration on checklist completion
- Change dismiss button to collapse chevron
- Update checklist action links to use query params for modals

* feat(designer): HF conenction

* feat(designer): add drag-and-drop file upload to onboarding wizard

- Add file upload capability to "Yep, ready to go!" onboarding option
- Create dataset and upload files automatically on wizard completion
- Hide upgrade banner during onboarding wizard flow
- Fix duplicate event dispatch with flag-based guard
- Dynamically fetch available strategies/databases for dataset creation
- Add debug logging throughout file upload flow

* feat(designer): add checklist navigator with contextual tips

- Add floating navigator that appears when following checklist links
- Show personalized tips based on project type, data status, and step
- Add "Done, next" button to mark steps complete and advance
- Fix Test page defaulting to wrong mode for doc-qa projects
- Make onboarding state properly project-specific via lf-active-project event
- Add loading state during project setup before wizard opens

* fix(designer): reset onboarding properly from demo and widen wizard

- Add removeDemoProject() to convert demo projects to regular projects
- Call removeDemoProject in resetOnboarding so "Build your own" works
- Widen wizard container to max-w-4xl for project type selector step
- Remove redundant width constraints from ProjectTypeSelector grid

* feat(designer): enhance onboarding flow for classifier/anomaly

- Add need-data checklists for classifier/anomaly with 3 steps
- Step 2 is now "Train & test" instead of separate create/test steps
- Fix ChecklistNavigator step matching (prefer exact path match)
- Add need-data tips in navigatorTips for classifier/anomaly
- Auto-open sample modal via URL param in ClassifierModel/AnomalyModel
- Auto-name model from selected sample dataset
- Show guidance message for classifier/anomaly has-data selection
- Add markdown link rendering in checklist descriptions
- Improve HF import with text field auto-detection
- Extract text content from HF datasets for better RAG processing
- Return detected_text_field, detected_label_field in import response

* feat(designer): add sample test inputs to classifier/anomaly demos

Add sampleQuestions to all 6 classifier/anomaly sample demos so users
know what to test after training:

- sentiment-classifier: positive/negative text examples
- expense-classifier: travel/meal expense examples
- fridge-temp-anomaly: normal (37.2) and anomalous (58.5) temps
- biometric-anomaly: normal and elevated health metrics
- build-status-anomaly: passing and critical failure CI/CD messages
- support-ticket-anomaly: normal and urgent ticket patterns

* feat(designer): improve onboarding checklist navigation and UX

- Add 2x2 grid layout for project type selector (hide doc-scan)
- Fix ChecklistNavigator z-index to appear above browser dev tools
- Support same-page step transitions in navigator (train/test on same page)
- Add Train button at top of classifier/anomaly model pages
- Auto-select correct mode and model on Test page via URL params
- Update need-data checklist step 2 to link to Test page instead of training
- Add "Tweak your prompt" step to exploring checklist
- Import file-based demos in background (stay on dashboard)

* refactor(designer): consolidate onboarding code and remove unused components

- Delete unused ChecklistItem.tsx and ChecklistEmptyState.tsx (~142 lines)
- Refactor checklistGenerator.ts with factory functions for Ship and Test steps
- Replace nested conditionals with MODEL_CHECKLIST_MAP lookup
- Remove commented template from demos.ts
- Clean up Dashboard.tsx: remove dead code and console.log statements
- Total reduction: ~367 lines without affecting user experience

* fix(designer): address PR review feedback

- Remove debug console.log statements from onboarding components
- Fix Enter key handler to ignore input/textarea elements
- Add type="button" to RadioSelector to prevent form submission
- Guard JSON.parse in useDemoWorkflow with try/catch
- Fix sample dataset example counts to match actual data
- Fix isFileBasedDemo type guard to check sampleQuestions
- Add max_rows lower bound validation in HF import
- Fix URL query param injection with proper encoding
- Use None instead of 0 for missing chunk_count
- Fix query param clearing to preserve other params
- Use less predictable window key for temp file storage
- Fix Data.tsx to use datasetService methods

* fix: address remaining PR review feedback

- Add refetchDatasets() after direct service call in Data.tsx
- Fix chunk_count to use dict.get() without or None (preserves 0)
- Sanitize filenames in huggingface.py to prevent path traversal
- Use regex to sanitize HF dataset ID in filename construction

* fix: correct sentiment dataset example count to 72

* fix(server): add path containment check for CodeQL security alert

Add explicit path validation to ensure symlink path stays within
the expected index directory, preventing path traversal attacks.
This satisfies CodeQL's security scanner by adding a runtime check
that verifies the normalized path starts with the expected prefix.

* fix(designer): remove HuggingFace dataset import feature

Removes the HuggingFace dataset import functionality to address
CodeQL security vulnerability (path traversal in file operations).

Users who need HF datasets can download them from huggingface.co
and upload files directly to LlamaFarm.

Changes:
- Remove server/api/routers/huggingface.py router
- Remove frontend HF API client and types
- Remove HFDatasetFinder component from onboarding
- Remove HF tab from ImportSampleDatasetModal
- Update Dashboard to remove HF import handling
- Simplify DataStatusSelector to link to HF instead of embed

* fix(designer): guard Enter key against contenteditable elements

* fix(designer): address security issues in onboarding

- Replace window storage pattern with React Context ref for file uploads
- Bundle canvas-confetti as dependency instead of loading from CDN

Security improvements:
- File objects now stored in useRef instead of window.__lf_onboarding_*
- Confetti library imported from npm package with SRI integrity

commit.message:
feat(server): add dynamic value substitution for prompts and tools (#672)

* feat(server): add dynamic value substitution for prompts and tools

Adds support for Jinja2-style template syntax ({{variable | default}})
in LlamaFarm configurations. Variables are passed via a new `variables`
field in ChatRequest and resolved at request time.

Features:
- TemplateService for regex-based template resolution
- Variables field in ChatRequest (OpenAI-compatible extension)
- Dynamic prompt resolution in PromptService
- Dynamic tool parameter resolution in ChatOrchestratorAgent
- Full backwards compatibility with existing configurations

Includes 124 unit tests covering edge cases and 4 example projects.

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Add warning log when variables provided but agent doesn't support substitution
- Add security note documenting trust model for templates and variables

* fix: address additional PR review feedback

- Add proper HTTP 400 error handling for TemplateError in API endpoint
- Add warning log before raising TemplateError for better debugging
- Add security edge case tests (injection attempts, long values)
- Add type coercion tests (int, float, bool, list, dict)
- Add test for default values containing closing brace

Total tests: 134 (10 new)

* fix(server): address PR review feedback for dynamic values

Fixes identified by cubic-dev-ai and claude reviews:

P1 (Critical):
- Add logger initialization in projects.py before warning block

P2 (Template resolution):
- Always resolve templates (even with empty variables) to apply defaults
- update_prompts_with_variables: Remove early return, always resolve
- update_config_tools_with_variables: Remove early return, always resolve
- get_resolved_config_tools: Always call TemplateService.resolve_object
- Request tools: Always resolve templates when tools are present

P2 (Test quality):
- test_rag_queries_not_affected: Replace tautology with actual test
  that verifies TemplateService behavior on RAG-like strings

All 420 tests pass.

* fix(server): add 'from e' to exception chaining for ruff B904

* feat(server): add input validation and improve edge case handling

Security improvements:
- Add MAX_VALUE_LENGTH (100KB) to prevent DoS via huge variable values
- Change None value handling: None -> "" (empty string) instead of "None" literal
- Log type coercion when converting non-string values
- Document threat model and limitations in TemplateService docstring

New tests (13 added, 433 total):
- TestValidationAndLimits: max length enforcement, empty variable names
- TestMalformedTemplatesAdditional: pipe without variable, nested markers
- TestSecurityValidation: HTML in values, unicode variable names
- Updated None value tests to expect empty string

The request tools code already follows immutability pattern - no mutation fix needed.

* refactor(server): simplify variable resolution with single agent method

Address PR review feedback:
- Add set_request_variables() method that resolves prompts and tools together
- Simplify API endpoint from ~40 lines to ~15 lines of orchestration code
- Move imports to top of files, remove inline imports
- Rename _model_config to _model_config_template for clarity
- Remove redundant initialization and sys.path hack
- Mark old update_*_with_variables methods as deprecated

* fix(server): add type validation and security tests for dynamic values

Address PR review feedback:
- Reject complex types (list, dict, custom classes) with clear error
- Add comprehensive security tests for nested template expansion
- Document variable value trust boundary in docstring
- Add tests for defaults containing closing brace
- Fix demo scripts to read PORT from .env file
- Fix demo scripts to use correct API endpoint path

commit.message:
chore: add Claude Code configuration and automation (#666)

* feat(claude): add Claude Code configuration and automation

Adds comprehensive Claude Code setup including:
- Agents for senior engineering, code simplification, and verification
- Skills for code review, commit automation, and config validation
- Hooks for code review enforcement and Python linting
- Rules defining project standards and component guidelines
- MCP server configuration for Playwright integration

Also adds config validation tooling and fixes a security utility.

* chore(claude): enhance skills with quality checks and simplification

- Add Simplification category to code-review with checks for duplicate
  logic, unnecessary complexity, and verbose patterns
- Add quality check step to commit-push-pr skill with auto-fix for
  debug statements and blocking checks for security issues
- Rename reports directory to reviews for clarity

commit.message:
feat(designer): add all api calls to dev tools (#680)

* feat(designer): dev tools start

* feat(designer): style changes

* fix(designer): updates to the dev tool drawer to test page

* fix(designer): address PR review feedback for DevTools

- Fix empty-string request/response bodies being suppressed
- Fix Python code gen boolean/null replacement with word boundaries
- Fix JavaScript code gen double-serializing string bodies
- Fix duplicate DevTools error capture in chatCompletionsService
- Add try/catch for localStorage read/write in DevToolsContext
- Fix streamComplete set unconditionally in updateResponse
- Remove duplicate StatusBadge/MethodBadge from DevToolsCollapsedBar
- Fix memory leak in DevToolsCodeSnippets setTimeout

* fix(designer): always JSON.stringify payload in JS code gen

Fixes issue where string bodies injected without quoting could produce
invalid JavaScript syntax.

* fix(designer): test chat loading state back to "thinking..."

* fix(designer): doc scanning was broken

* feat(designer): capture all API calls in DevTools via axios interceptor

Add global DevTools capture for all axios API calls using an event emitter pattern:

- Add devToolsEmitter.ts: pub/sub emitter that axios interceptors emit to
- Add axios request/response interceptors in client.ts to capture all API calls
- Subscribe DevToolsContext to emitter events for automatic capture
- Handle FormData bodies specially (show file info instead of serializing)
- Fix GET method color for better dark mode accessibility (sky-300)

Previously only chat completion calls were captured. Now all ~40 API calls
(ML, datasets, models, projects, health, etc.) automatically appear in DevTools.

* feat(vision): add parse_by_page option for document scanning

- Add parse_by_page parameter to OCR endpoint (default: false)
- When false, combine multi-page PDFs into single result with averaged confidence
- Track pages_combined in response for UI differentiation
- Add "Parse by page" checkbox to document scanning settings
- Show "Full (N pg)" badge when pages combined vs "N pg" for per-page results

* fix(designer): remove redundant DevTools capture and improve code gen escaping

- Remove manual DevTools capture from chatCompletionsService (axios interceptors handle it)
- Remove useDevToolsCapture from useChatCompletions hooks
- Add proper escaping for URLs and headers in cURL generation
- Add case-insensitive header lookup for Content-Type checks
- Add escaping for Python and JavaScript code generation

* fix(designer): properly convert JSON to Python literals in code gen

Replace regex-based null/true/false replacement with recursive
toPythonLiteral() function that properly handles type conversion
without corrupting string values that may contain these words.

commit.message:
fix(runtime): move deps to main and enable offline GGUF loading (#670)

* fix(runtime): move deps to main and enable offline GGUF loading

Two fixes to improve universal-runtime reliability:

1. Move optional dependencies to main:
   - scikit-learn (anomaly detection)
   - setfit, sentence-transformers (classification)
   - surya-ocr, easyocr, opencv-python-headless, pytesseract (OCR)

   Prevents ModuleNotFoundError when users run without extras.

2. Enable offline GGUF model loading:
   - Check local HuggingFace cache before making network calls
   - Fall back gracefully to cache if network fails
   - Enables air-gapped systems and intermittent connectivity (e.g., Jetson Nano)

* feat(runtime): add full llama.cpp parameter passthrough for Jetson optimization

Add support for passing critical llama.cpp parameters from YAML config
through to the model initialization, enabling fine-grained control for
memory-constrained devices like Jetson Orin Nano.

New parameters that now pass through from extra_body:
- n_batch: batch size for prompt processing (reduces compute buffer)
- n_threads: CPU thread count for inference
- use_mmap: memory-mapped file loading for efficient swapping
- use_mlock: RAM locking control for OS memory management

Also adds Memory Guard logic that automatically reduces n_batch to 512
when available system memory is below 3GB, preventing CUDA OOM errors.

Example Jetson Orin Nano config:
  extra_body:
    n_ctx: 2048
    n_batch: 512      # Reduces compute buffer from 1.2GB to ~300MB
    n_gpu_layers: 29  # All layers on GPU
    n_threads: 6      # Match 6-core CPU
    flash_attn: true  # Ampere optimizations
    use_mmap: true    # Efficient swapping
    use_mlock: false  # Allow OS memory management

* fix(security): add path traversal validation and fix test coverage

- Add _validate_model_id() to prevent path traversal in cache functions
- Validate model_id format matches HuggingFace pattern (org/repo or repo)
- Validate filename in _get_cached_gguf_path() has no path separators
- Fix test_get_gguf_file_path_fallback_to_cache_on_network_error to
  actually test the fallback path by having first cache lookup fail

Addresses security review comments from qodo and claude agents.
Fixes test coverage issue identified by cubic-dev-ai.

* feat(runtime): add KV cache quantization passthrough (cache_type_k/v)

Add full passthrough for cache_type_k and cache_type_v parameters from
YAML config through to llama.cpp. These parameters allow quantizing the
KV cache to reduce memory usage on constrained devices like Jetson.

Setting both to "q4_0" can reduce KV cache memory by ~4x, which is
critical for running larger models on 8GB devices.

Changes:
- llamafarm-llama: Add cache_type_k/v params to Llama class with GGML
  type enum mapping (f32, f16, q4_0, q4_1, q5_0, q5_1, q8_0)
- gguf_language_model.py: Accept and forward cache_type_k/v to Llama()
- types.py: Add cache_type_k/v to ChatCompletionRequest
- service.py: Extract cache_type_k/v from request and extra_body
- server.py: Update cache key and load_language() signature
- test_cache_keys.py: Add tests for cache_type_k/v parameters

Example Jetson config:
  extra_body:
    n_ctx: 2048
    n_batch: 512
    cache_type_k: q4_0  # Quantize KV cache keys
    cache_type_v: q4_0  # Quantize KV cache values

* docs(schema): add GGUF/llama.cpp parameters to config schema

Document all GGUF model parameters in extra_body schema:
- n_batch: batch size for prompt processing (memory optimization)
- n_gpu_layers: GPU layer offloading control
- n_threads: CPU thread count
- flash_attn: flash attention toggle
- use_mmap: memory-mapped file loading
- use_mlock: RAM lock setting
- cache_type_k/v: KV cache quantization (enum validated)

Schema keeps additionalProperties: true to allow pass-through
of any other provider-specific parameters.

* docs(schema): remove enum constraints from cache_type_k/v for flexibility

Allow any value to be passed through while documenting common options.

* fix: address PR review feedback and add GGUF documentation

- Fix TOCTOU vulnerability in _get_cached_gguf_files() and _get_cached_gguf_path()
  by using single getsize() call instead of exists() then getsize()
- Add path traversal validation tests (7 new tests)
- Update test_model_format.py to mock local cache for API path testing
- Add tests for local cache detection behavior (2 new tests)
- Add comprehensive GGUF parameters documentation including:
  - Parameter reference table (n_ctx, n_batch, n_gpu_layers, etc.)
  - Jetson Orin Nano optimized configuration example
  - Memory estimation table
  - Memory Guard and offline operation documentation
- Add deprecation comment for duplicate extras in pyproject.toml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): fix go-jsonschema bug with additionalProperties + minimum

When go-jsonschema generates code for objects with both additionalProperties:true
and minimum constraints on properties, it produces code that uses a 'raw' variable
without declaring it. This causes compilation errors.

The fix adds a post-processing step in generate-types.sh that detects this pattern
and inserts the missing variable declaration and unmarshal call.

Affected schema property: extra_body (has additionalProperties:true with minimum
constraints on n_batch, n_gpu_layers, n_threads)

* fix(common): use sorted() for deterministic GGUF file ordering

When returning cached GGUF files, use sorted() instead of list() to ensure
deterministic ordering. This prevents select_gguf_file from potentially
picking different files between runs due to set iteration order being
non-deterministic.

Addresses cubic-dev-ai P2 review comment.

* fix(common): filter out split GGUF files when non-split version exists

Add is_split_gguf_file() function to detect split GGUF files (those with
-00001-of-00002 pattern). Update select_gguf_file() to prefer non-split
files over split files when both exist for the same quantization.

This fixes an issue where split file parts could be incorrectly selected
instead of the proper single-file version (e.g., selecting
qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m-00001-of-00002.gguf instead of
qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf).

Split files are still used when they're the only option for a requested
quantization (e.g., F16 which may be too large for a single file).

* fix(tests): update model unloading tests for new cache key format

Update test_load_language_tracks_access and test_model_reaccess_updates_timestamp
to use the full cache key format that includes all parameters:
language:{model_id}:ctx{ctx}:batch{batch}:gpu{gpu}:threads{threads}:flash{flash}:mmap{mmap}:mlock{mlock}:cachek{k}:cachev{v}:quant{quant}

The tests were using the old shorter format which didn't match the actual
cache key generated by _make_language_cache_key().

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix OCR: use None for unknown confidence instead of misleading 0.9 default

## [0.0.28](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/compare/v0.0.27...v0.0.28) (2026-03-05)


### Features

* **cli:** add deploy command and bundle packaging ([#772](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/772)) ([da1bb13](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/da1bb13b8429bd044712f7f8aed023cbac655e5f))
* **designer:** Bundle UI for deployment workflow ([#783](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/783)) ([906b9e3](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/906b9e31c6be48acc330ef2156616032e7d11169))
* ML addons — timeseries, ADTK, drift, CatBoost (addon-based, cherry-pickable) ([#766](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/766)) ([55850d6](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/55850d6dae7c28450d5fc15b1e9c12f6ee434c97))
* **runtime:** add logprobs support for GGUF chat completions ([#791](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/791)) ([79c9874](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/79c98741b031d1dd138e38bba3a8b88017bb8642))
* Server-Side KV Cache with Multi-Turn Chaining and Pre-Warming ([#782](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/782)) ([e750ed1](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/e750ed19b5d25d83ca2c66173719844533576d9f))
* **server:** add builtin tools system with tasks tool ([#738](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/738)) ([952597c](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/952597c51a33799b64001ded3dd3b1c8196ede8b))
* support structured outputs ([#724](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/724)) ([55fa703](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/55fa703702cc18a167fc6521a8bfd1f548e3ba4e))
* vision API improvements, evaluation pipeline, and object tracking ([#780](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/780)) ([f9635a4](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/f9635a476e118cec8f79bd49ae00c5c1d3feb1f5))
* Vision UI for Designer ([#777](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/777)) ([6542c74](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/6542c7474a6d8a65af7790b0edee23992893e5e6))
* **vision:** detect+classify combo, session TTL cleanup, COCO dataset support ([#774](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/774)) ([96151ab](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/96151ab2ae3d9cf7b0634b4109d7a30635d29984))
* **vision:** Vision MVP — detect, classify, train, cascade, feedback ([#765](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/765)) ([bda3c6e](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/bda3c6e1d31444e4ba20c64ffa4b0f90abb1be44))


### Bug Fixes

* **ci:** read prose changelog from file to avoid ARG_MAX overflow ([ccae0a0](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/ccae0a0c4402ddd45ef782d317b909d2b971abf3))
* **cli:** embed addon registry into binary for released builds ([#792](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/792)) ([38b481e](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/38b481e5219c0ec56cefadd116b08d4b7fa42dcc))
* content budget math ([#779](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/779)) ([84e05bc](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/84e05bc410ae0f5b1ab739a0d79b2a92965b63a7))
* **designer:** remote access ([#750](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/750)) ([e35b050](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/e35b050fe72ea5909706d03a1a317d79b68ad034))
* exclude base-install dependencies from addon wheel bundles ([#770](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/770)) ([c72f0dd](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/c72f0dd2552ee57cd3587f786b02399aad04a640))
* return 422 for unavailable timeseries backends instead of 500 ([#787](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/787)) ([ce4f933](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/ce4f933cdc1fa6daf9c7f26d33dd328eca6849e9))


### Miscellaneous Chores

* release 0.0.28 ([91c924d](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/91c924de8588456c3eaba89571e14448386d3c7a))

## Desktop App Downloads

### macOS (Apple Silicon only)
Download for Apple Silicon (M1+) Macs:
- **Apple Silicon (arm64)**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-mac-arm64.dmg` or `.zip`

**Installation:**
1. Download the DMG or ZIP file
2. Open the DMG and drag LlamaFarm to Applications (or extract the ZIP)
3. Launch the app (it's signed and notarized, so it will open normally)
4. The app will auto-install the CLI and start services

### Linux
Download the appropriate package for your Linux distribution:
- **AppImage** (Universal): `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage`
- **Debian/Ubuntu**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.deb`

**Installation:**
```bash
# AppImage (works on all distributions)
chmod +x LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage
./LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage

# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo dpkg -i LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.deb
```

### Windows
Download the Windows installer:
- **Windows Installer**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-windows.exe`

**Installation:**
1. Download the installer
2. Run the installer (it will install to your user directory)
3. Launch LlamaFarm from the Start Menu
4. The app will auto-install the CLI and start services

---

### Auto-Updates
Once installed, the app will automatically check for updates and prompt you to install them.

## [0.0.25](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/compare/v0.0.24...v0.0.25) (2026-01-14)


### Features

* auto-process files on dataset upload ([#661](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/661)) ([e08d762](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/e08d762d73baf128023ab571352c88b8e88a1533))
* **designer:** add all api calls to dev tools ([#680](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/680)) ([d36f6ff](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/d36f6ffb9967d9e43027c7761c2395d5ca94b4ee))
* **designer:** add sse streaming for embedding model downloads ([#655](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/655)) ([b2ffbd0](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/b2ffbd0319f6bab7c165fd78a1c87cbfc9738833))
* **designer:** test space to include anomaly and classifier tests +other updates  ([#657](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/657)) ([fc94563](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/fc94563cbcde16614dddb6bcddc4a9c0fe93077d))
* **runtime:** support for native tool calling ([#636](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/636)) ([f99f305](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/f99f30538ffc4d42be46ba1cfd43ef1cc758f935))


### Bug Fixes

* **cli:** config validation errors output ([#673](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/673)) ([6d92f43](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/6d92f43913cc5cbd135516a624b20900f4c55970))
* install/run failures on windows w/ nvidia ([#665](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/665)) ([29f3242](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/29f324246040d1db6cc45faf570fdd60d0396cce))
* **rag:** remove parser fallback ([#642](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/642)) ([7037219](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/7037219de690a4518bf5acb16afe730d390faa82))
* **runtime:** move deps to main and enable offline GGUF loading ([#670](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/issues/670)) ([1d9de0c](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/1d9de0ce9cc80c1ad0bdf4bdbbfef8af75c3c21d))


### Miscellaneous Chores

* release 0.0.25 ([74dea58](https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/commit/74dea58befffae34d9a3ac1072c680b3e20b5ea5))

---

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

## Desktop App Downloads

### macOS
Download for Apple Silicon (M1+) or Intel Macs:
- **Apple Silicon (arm64)**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-mac-arm64.dmg` or `.zip`
- **Intel (x64)**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-mac-x64.dmg` or `.zip`

**Installation:**
1. Download the DMG or ZIP file for your architecture
2. Open the DMG and drag LlamaFarm to Applications (or extract the ZIP)
3. Launch the app (it's signed and notarized, so it will open normally)
4. The app will auto-install the CLI and start services

### Linux
Download the appropriate package for your Linux distribution:
- **AppImage** (Universal): `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage`
- **Debian/Ubuntu**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.deb`

**Installation:**
```bash
# AppImage (works on all distributions)
chmod +x LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage
./LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.AppImage

# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo dpkg -i LlamaFarm-desktop-app-linux.deb
```

### Windows
Download the Windows installer:
- **Windows Installer**: `LlamaFarm-desktop-app-windows.exe`

**Installation:**
1. Download the installer
2. Run the installer (it will install to your user directory)
3. Launch LlamaFarm from the Start Menu
4. The app will auto-install the CLI and start services

---

### Auto-Updates
Once installed, the app will automatically check for updates and prompt you to install them.

readme_html:
LlamaFarm - Edge AI for Everyone

Enterprise AI capabilities on your own hardware. No cloud required.

LlamaFarm is an open-source AI platform that runs entirely on your hardware. Build RAG applications, train custom classifiers, detect anomalies, and run document processing—all locally with complete privacy.

  • 🔒 Complete Privacy — Your data never leaves your device
  • 💰 No API Costs — Use open-source models without per-token fees
  • 🌐 Offline Capable — Works without internet once models are downloaded
  • ⚡ Hardware Optimized — Automatic GPU/NPU acceleration on Apple Silicon, NVIDIA, and AMD

Desktop App Downloads

Get started instantly — no command line required:

Platform Download
Mac (Universal) Download
Windows Download
Linux (x86_64) Download
Linux (ARM64) Download

What Can You Build?

Capability Description
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Ingest PDFs, docs, CSVs and query them with AI
Custom Classifiers Train text classifiers with 8-16 examples using SetFit
Anomaly Detection 12+ algorithms for batch and streaming anomaly detection
Tool Calling (MCP) Connect models to external tools via Model Context Protocol
OCR & Document Extraction Extract text and structured data from images and PDFs
Named Entity Recognition Find people, organizations, and locations
Multi-Model Runtime Switch between Ollama, OpenAI, vLLM, or local GGUF models

Video demo (90 seconds): https://youtu.be/W7MHGyN0MdQ

Quickstart

Option 1: Desktop App

Download the desktop app above and run it. No additional setup required.

Option 2: CLI + Development Mode

  1. Install the CLI

    macOS / Linux:

    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/main/install.sh | bash

    Windows (PowerShell):

    irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llama-farm/llamafarm/main/install.ps1 | iex

    Or download directly from releases.

  2. Create and run a project

    lf init my-project      # Generates llamafarm.yaml
lf start                # Starts services and opens Designer UI

  3. Chat with your AI

    lf chat                           # Interactive chat
lf chat "Hello, LlamaFarm!"       # One-off message

The Designer web interface is available at http://localhost:14345.

Option 3: Development from Source

git clone https://github.com/llama-farm/llamafarm.git
cd llamafarm

# Install Nx globally and initialize the workspace
npm install -g nx
nx init --useDotNxInstallation --interactive=false  # Required on first clone

# Start all services (run each in a separate terminal)
nx start server           # FastAPI server (port 14345)
nx start rag              # RAG worker for document processing
nx start universal-runtime # ML models, OCR, embeddings (port 11540)

Architecture

LlamaFarm consists of three main services:

Service Port Purpose
Server 14345 FastAPI REST API, Designer web UI, project management
RAG Worker - Celery worker for async document processing
Universal Runtime 11540 ML model inference, embeddings, OCR, anomaly detection

All configuration lives in llamafarm.yaml—no scattered settings or hidden defaults.

Runtime Options

Universal Runtime (Recommended)

The Universal Runtime provides access to HuggingFace models plus specialized ML capabilities:

  • Text Generation - Any HuggingFace text model
  • Embeddings - sentence-transformers and other embedding models
  • OCR - Text extraction from images/PDFs (Surya, EasyOCR, PaddleOCR, Tesseract)
  • Document Extraction - Forms, invoices, receipts via vision models
  • Text Classification - Pre-trained or custom models via SetFit
  • Named Entity Recognition - Extract people, organizations, locations
  • Reranking - Cross-encoder models for improved RAG quality
  • Anomaly Detection - Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM, Local Outlier Factor, Autoencoders
runtime:
  models:
    default:
      provider: universal
      model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct
      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11540/v1

Ollama

Simple setup for GGUF models with CPU/GPU acceleration:

runtime:
  models:
    default:
      provider: ollama
      model: qwen3:8b
      base_url: http://localhost:11434/v1

OpenAI-Compatible

Works with vLLM, Together, Mistral API, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

runtime:
  models:
    default:
      provider: openai
      model: gpt-4o
      base_url: https://api.openai.com/v1
      api_key: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}

Core Workflows

CLI Commands

Task Command
Initialize project lf init my-project
Start services lf start
Interactive chat lf chat
One-off message lf chat "Your question"
List models lf models list
Use specific model lf chat --model powerful "Question"
Create dataset lf datasets create -s pdf_ingest -b main_db research
Upload files (auto-process by default) lf datasets upload research ./docs/*.pdf
Process dataset (if you skipped auto-process) lf datasets process research
Query RAG lf rag query --database main_db "Your query"
Check RAG health lf rag health

RAG Pipeline

  1. Create a dataset linked to a processing strategy and database
  2. Upload files (PDF, DOCX, Markdown, TXT) — processing runs automatically unless you pass --no-process
  3. Process manually only when you intentionally skipped auto-processing (e.g., large batches)
  4. Query using semantic search with optional metadata filtering
lf datasets create -s default -b main_db research
lf datasets upload research ./papers/*.pdf                 # auto-processes by default
# For large batches:
# lf datasets upload research ./papers/*.pdf --no-process
# lf datasets process research
lf rag query --database main_db "What are the key findings?"

Designer Web UI

The Designer at http://localhost:14345 provides:

  • Project management with briefs and quick actions
  • Visual dataset management with drag-and-drop uploads
  • Database & RAG configuration with built-in query testing
  • Prompt engineering with template variables and testing
  • Interactive chat with RAG toggle and retrieved context display
  • Config editor with syntax highlighting, validation, and auto-completion
  • Switch between visual Designer and raw YAML modes in any section

See the Designer Features Guide for details.

Configuration

llamafarm.yaml is the source of truth for each project:

version: v1
name: my-assistant
namespace: default

# Multi-model configuration
runtime:
  default_model: fast

  models:
    fast:
      description: "Fast local model"
      provider: universal
      model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct
      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11540/v1

    powerful:
      description: "More capable model"
      provider: universal
      model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct
      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11540/v1

# System prompts
prompts:
  - name: default
    messages:
      - role: system
        content: You are a helpful assistant.

# RAG configuration
rag:
  databases:
    - name: main_db
      type: ChromaStore
      default_embedding_strategy: default_embeddings
      default_retrieval_strategy: semantic_search
      embedding_strategies:
        - name: default_embeddings
          type: UniversalEmbedder
          config:
            model: sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
            base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11540/v1
      retrieval_strategies:
        - name: semantic_search
          type: BasicSimilarityStrategy
          config:
            top_k: 5

  data_processing_strategies:
    - name: default
      parsers:
        - type: PDFParser_LlamaIndex
          config:
            chunk_size: 1000
            chunk_overlap: 100
        - type: MarkdownParser_Python
          config:
            chunk_size: 1000
      extractors: []

# Dataset definitions
datasets:
  - name: research
    data_processing_strategy: default
    database: main_db

Environment Variable Substitution

Use ${VAR} syntax to inject secrets from .env files:

runtime:
  models:
    openai:
      api_key: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      # With default: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-sk-default}
      # From specific file: ${file:.env.production:API_KEY}

See the Configuration Guide for complete reference.

REST API

LlamaFarm provides an OpenAI-compatible REST API:

Chat Completions

curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/projects/default/my-project/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
    "stream": false,
    "rag_enabled": true
  }'

RAG Query

curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/projects/default/my-project/rag/query \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "What are the requirements?",
    "database": "main_db",
    "top_k": 5
  }'

See the API Reference for all endpoints.

Specialized ML Capabilities

The Universal Runtime provides endpoints beyond chat:

OCR & Document Extraction

curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/vision/ocr \
  -F "file=@document.pdf" \
  -F "model=surya"

Anomaly Detection

LlamaFarm supports 12+ anomaly detection algorithms via PyOD, with both batch and streaming modes.

# Train on normal data
curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/ml/anomaly/fit \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "sensor-detector", "backend": "ecod", "data": [[22.1], [23.5], ...]}'

# Detect anomalies
curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/ml/anomaly/detect \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "sensor-detector", "data": [[22.0], [100.0], [23.0]], "threshold": 0.5}'

# Streaming detection (handles cold start, auto-retraining, sliding windows)
curl -X POST http://localhost:14345/v1/ml/anomaly/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "live-sensor", "data": {"temperature": 72.5}, "backend": "ecod"}'

Available backends: ecod (recommended), isolation_forest, one_class_svm, local_outlier_factor, autoencoder, hbos, copod, knn, mcd, cblof, suod, loda

Text Classification & NER

See the Models Guide for complete documentation.

Tool Calling (MCP)

Give models access to external tools via the Model Context Protocol:

# In llamafarm.yaml
mcp:
  servers:
    - name: filesystem
      transport: stdio
      command: npx
      args: ['-y', '@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem', '/data']

runtime:
  models:
    - name: assistant
      provider: ollama
      model: llama3.1:8b
      mcp_servers: [filesystem]

LlamaFarm also exposes its own API as MCP tools for use with Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other MCP clients. See the Tool Calling Guide.

Examples

Example Description Location
RAG Examples
Large Complex PDFs Multi-megabyte planning ordinances examples/large_complex_rag/
Many Small Files FDA correspondence letters examples/many_small_file_rag/
Mixed Formats PDF, Markdown, HTML, text, and code examples/mixed_format_rag/
Quick Notes Rapid smoke tests with small files examples/quick_rag/
Anomaly Detection
Quick Start Simplest anomaly detection example examples/anomaly/01_quick_start.py
Fraud Detection Training, saving, loading models examples/anomaly/02_fraud_detection.py
Streaming Sensors IoT monitoring with rolling features examples/anomaly/03_streaming_sensors.py
Backend Comparison Compare all 12 algorithms examples/anomaly/04_backend_comparison.py
Use Cases
FDA Letters Assistant Regulatory document analysis examples/fda_rag/
Government Planning Large ordinance documents examples/gov_rag/

See examples/README.md for setup instructions and the full list.

Industry Use Cases

LlamaFarm is used across industries for document analysis, monitoring, and fraud detection:

  • Pharmaceutical & Therapeutics — Analyze FDA correspondence, track regulatory questions
  • IoT Sensor Monitoring — Real-time streaming anomaly detection with automatic retraining
  • Financial Fraud Detection — Multi-stage fraud detection with velocity and behavioral patterns

Development & Testing

# Python server tests
cd server && uv sync && uv run --group test python -m pytest

# CLI tests
cd cli && go test ./...

# RAG tests
cd rag && uv sync && uv run pytest tests/

# Universal Runtime tests
cd runtimes/universal && uv sync && uv run pytest tests/

# Build docs
nx build docs

Extensibility

  • Add runtimes by implementing provider support and updating schema
  • Add vector stores by implementing store backends (Chroma, Qdrant, etc.)
  • Add parsers for new file formats (PDF, DOCX, HTML, CSV, etc.)
  • Add extractors for custom metadata extraction
  • Add CLI commands under cli/cmd/

See the Extending Guide for step-by-step instructions.

Community & Support

  • Discord - Chat with the team and community
  • GitHub Issues - Bug reports and feature requests
  • Discussions - Ideas and proposals
  • Contributing Guide - Code style and contribution process
